events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description:
A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level.

Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath.

\*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged

\* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing.

Some benefits of breathwork include:

- Connect to your creativity

- Increase Self Love and appreciation

- Stress relief

- Increased Self Awareness

- Ease symptoms of Depression

- Ease symptoms of Anxiety

- Peace of mind

- Open the heart

- Increased Intuition

- Relaxation

- Release trauma and fear

- Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings

10-Class Pass Rate: $11

Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: Park City Film Presents: NATCHEZ
events.description:
"Natchez" captures an unsettling clash between history and memory in a small Mississippi town; a layered mosaic of people contending with the weight of the past in a place where it is always present.

Equal parts amusing and disturbing, we journey through an antebellum tourist destination at a crossroads as it grapples with a deeply troubled history that is so thoroughly ingrained in its present, we're left to wonder if it's actually past at all.

"The genteel politeness on display at the start of the film falls away, revealing an unsettling core" -HOLLYWOOD REPORTER

Post-film Q&A with director Suzannah Herbert.

Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) - a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

More information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Remaining Native
events.description:
Ku Stevens \(17\) is the solo runner at his high school with no coach. Living on the Yerington Paiute reservation in Northwest Nevada he needs more to be seen by his dream school, the University of Oregon. As Ku trains, unreconciled emotions unearth the memory of his great-grandfather, Frank Quinn. At 8 years old, Frank ran 50 miles across the desert to escape an Indian boarding school. Frank's story becomes interwoven withKu's journey to run a collegiate qualifying time. Will Ku outrun his history or will he learn to run in parallel with it to achieve his dreams?

Post-film Q&A with director Paige Bethmann.

Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) - a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

events.title: Jylian Gustlin + Lola: Exhibition
events.description:
Gallery MAR is pleased to present a summer exhibition featuring new work by Lola and Jylian Gustlin, opening **July 3rd** with an artist reception from **6–9 pm** at the gallery’s Main Street location in Park City. On view for **three weeks**, the exhibition brings together two distinctive contemporary artists whose practices explore abstraction through color, material, and perception.

Lola, a self-taught visual artist based in the San Francisco Bay Area, is best known for her intensely vibrant abstractions created with tinted epoxy resin. Working on a raised horizontal surface, she allows the resin to flow, pool, and spill over the edges of the panel, creating luminous compositions framed by gravity-defying drips that have become a signature of her work. Her newest series, *Quiet Places*, marks an evolution in her practice—moving away from densely layered forms toward simpler shapes, softer palettes, and shadowy overlays that invite viewers to experience moments of calm and serenity. For Lola, color remains the central language of emotion, and each work is intended to create an experience that allows viewers to find their own meaning and sense of optimism within the composition.

In conversation with Lola’s emotive abstractions are the richly layered paintings of Jylian Gustlin. Influenced by the San Francisco Bay Area Figurative painters and informed by her background in computer science and digital graphics programming, Gustlin’s work explores the intersection of art, mathematics, and perception. Drawing on concepts such as the Fibonacci sequence and entropy, she creates complex compositions using oil, acrylic, charcoal, wax, gold leaf, pastel, and graphite. Gustlin builds her surfaces through drawing, painting, and scratching into the material itself, resulting in paintings that feel almost sculptural—dynamic fields of gesture, symbol, and color that encourage viewers to look closely and discover new relationships within the work.

Together, Lola and Gustlin...
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description:
Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever.

Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid.

In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. Light travels through the surface rather than simply reflecting off it, making each piece feel as much like a living object as a painting.

Join us August 7th to experience new works by Michael Kessler at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description:
Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him.

Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next.

The result is painting that moves the way wilderness does, familiar and elusive at once, inviting you closer while always suggesting there's something just beyond the next ridge.

Join us September 5th to experience new works by Matt Flint at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 national Day of Service and Remembrance
events.description:
Save the Date for Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 National Day of Service and Remembrance. The day starts at 8 a.m. in City Park with a complimentary breakfast for all volunteers, followed by a short program that honors 9/11/01. At 9 a.m. all volunteers head to their Serve Park City service projects they have signed up for on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity that are being held on 9/19/26. (These projects will be posted by August 1, 2026) Last year we had 35 different projects all over the Park City area that ran from 9 a.m. -12 noon with over 1200 volunteers participating. We invite all to serve. 

This event is hosted by Serve Park City, Inc. A nonprofit whose mission is to build community through service. Each year we partner with over 30 nonprofits and organizations in hosting a day of service, but find opportunities to serve throughout the year on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity. Learn more about us at serveparkcity.org...

events.title: Last Friday Gallery Stroll
events.description:
Hosted by the Park City Gallery Association, the Last Friday Gallery Stroll, is a fun \(and free\) monthly community event dedicated to highlighting artists and special exhibits at art galleries throughout town. Park City's art scene continues to thrive, offering a robust variety of unique art collections from local, national, and international artists. On the last Friday of each month, seventeen of our art galleries open their doors for visitors and locals alike to enjoy light refreshments and to admire their latest art installations.

Park City Gallery Stroll runs from 6:00 - 9:00pm on the last Friday of each month. It's the perfect way to explore the Park City art scene while enjoying scenic Historic Main Street and our beautiful alpine evenings....
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: Savor the Summit
events.description: Dine al fresco on Park City's Historic Main Street — Deer Valley returns to Savor the Summit for an unforgettable evening of open-air dining on Park City's Historic Main Street. The evening begins with a social hour featuring champagne pours from Laurent-Perrier and gourmet appetizers. Then, gather around our signature eight-foot paella pan, featuring squid ink rice with lobster, razor clams, octopus, and shrimp, as Deer Valley’s culinary team prepares a three-course dinner before your eyes. Paired with select wines from Duckhorn Vineyards, this is an experience you won't want to miss. Limited seating available. Secure your spot today. View Menu Reserve Now
events.location: Historic Park City Main Street, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Guided Historical Hike
events.description: Discover Park City's mining history — Step into Park City’s past on a guided hike through Deer Valley’s scenic terrain, where fresh mountain air meets stories of the area’s rich mining history. This three- to four-hour hike covers moderate terrain, typically under six miles, with sweeping views around Little Baldy. Meet at Snow Park Lodge, by the Stein Eriksen statue, for an 8:30 a.m. departure.
events.location: On Mountain, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Twilight Ride Series
events.description: Enjoy Extended Lift Hours for Mountain Biking — Experience the magic of the mountain at dusk this summer with our Twilight Ride Series! Join us every Wednesday, from June 24 to August 19, weather and conditions permitting, for lift-served mountain biking from 4 to 8 p.m. Take the Silver Lake Express, Homestake Express, or Sterling Express chairlifts and explore the trails in the evening glow.
events.location: On Mountain, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Live Music at Silver Lake

events.title: Overland Cookery Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. Led by Overland Cookery’s expedition chefs, this weekend draws inspiration from cooking in remote environments. Open fire techniques and thoughtful preparation create an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, it is an easy, social setting to spend the afternoon, where food, fire, and the outdoors come together. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Lyle Lovett
events.description:
Lyle Lovett is an americana singer-songwriter and actor from Klein, Texas, who has been described as "the thinking man's cowboy".  While typically associated with the alt-country genre, his albums typically incorporate folk, swing, blues, jazz and gospel music as well as more traditional country & western styles.

 

 The four-time Grammy winner has acted in a number of films and been a guest-star on television.  Lovett was married to actress Julia Roberts for a short time in the mid-1990s.  In 2002, he was trampled by a bull while working on his uncle's farm in Texas, but soon recovered and resumed touring....

events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Ready for that “razzle dazzle?” Experience the sensational story of Roxie Hart, Velma Kelly, and the infamous Merry Murderesses of the Cook County Jail in a tale of fame, scandal, and show business. Bring the picnic and your party for a show-stopping performance of the complete musical. In this concert event, Broadway vocalists join the Utah Symphony to bring John Kander and Fred Ebb’s iconic score to life in its entirety—don’t miss out on hearing the songs you know and love under the stars, including “All That Jazz,” “Cell Block Tango,” and “Mr. Cellophane.” Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.

events.title: Mountain Pride Day
events.description: Celebrate Pride in the Mountains — Join Deer Valley for our third annual Mountain Pride Day, a family-friendly celebration of Pride Month at Silver Lake Lawn. Enjoy a day of community, entertainment, and mountain experiences as we come together to celebrate inclusivity, connection, and belonging. Mountain Pride Day is free to attend. Guests may access Silver Lake Lawn via the Silver Lake Express chairlift from Snow Park with a scenic ride or mountain bike lift ticket. Additional event details and programming will be announced soon. Deer Valley will donate a percentage of all lift ticket sales on Mountain Pride Day to Summit Pride. Summit Pride works to sustain and amplify LGBTQ+ inclusivity in the Wasatch Back, ensuring everyone feels a sense of belonging.

events.title: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — If there’s one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it’s Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the “Ode to Joy,” as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner—an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven’s Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season. July 17 | Gates Open: 6:30 p.m. | Show Time: 8 p.m. July 18 | Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite & String Trio “Time for Three”
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite takes flight in this spectacular program led by Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner. First, GRAMMY®-winning trio Time for Three—known for their genre-defying blend of classical, Americana, and contemporary styles— takes the stage for Kevin Puts’ Contact, the winner of a GRAMMY® for Best Contemporary Classical Composition. Written specifically for the group by the Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, the work explores themes of human connection and highlights the trio’s dynamic, longtime partnership with the Utah Symphony. Then, hear one of the most thrilling pieces ever written echoing through the Wasatch Mountains as the orchestra takes you on a journey from the explosive “Infernal Dance” to the powerful finale of Stravinsky’s Firebird. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Chris Botti
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Spread out your picnic blanket and settle in for a smooth summer evening with GRAMMY® Award-winning trumpeter Chris Botti as he joins the Utah Symphony. Blending jazz, pop, and classical influences, Botti’s expressive playing captivates audiences around the world. Over the past three decades, he has recorded and performed with some of the biggest names in music—including Sting, Barbra Streisand, Tony Bennett, Yo-Yo Ma, and Andrea Bocelli—earning a reputation as one of the most beloved instrumentalists working today. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Reception & Five-Course Dinner
events.description: The Taste of Luxury Series featuring Stag's Leap — Deer Valley’s Taste of Luxury Series continues with a celebration honoring the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Judgment of Paris, the landmark tasting that forever changed the global perception of California wine and established Napa Valley as a world-class wine region. To commemorate the occasion, Deer Valley welcomes Marcus Notaro, head winemaker of Stag's Leap Wine Cellars, alongside returning guest Chef Clement Gelas of Talisker Club for an intimate evening at Deer Valley Café. Set pond-side at sunset, guests will enjoy a thoughtfully curated five-course pairing experience inspired by the wines, stories, and legacy behind this groundbreaking moment in wine history. RESERVE YOUR TICKETS EVENT SCHEDULE Meet-and-Greet Reception 6:30 p.m. Five-Course Dinner & Wine Pairing 7 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Reception, Dinner & Concert
events.description: The Taste of Luxury Series featuring Chef Ryan Arensdorf — Indulge in an unforgettable evening of Kansas City-style BBQ as Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to Deer Valley for the fourth annual Taste of Luxury: Smoked Series on August 7, 2026. Set against the stunning backdrop of Silver Lake Lawn, guests will have the opportunity to mingle with Chef Arensdorf before enjoying his signature smoked specialties alongside curated cocktails and pairings from High West Distillery and Offset Bier. Enhance the evening with an optional ticket to Chicago the Musical in Concert at the Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater. events.title: Fox US Open of Mountain Biking
events.description: The Official US National Mountain Bike Championship — The Fox US Open of Mountain Biking is coming to Deer Valley! Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Pro Finals will take place on Saturday, September 12. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.

events.title: Chef Sarah Glover Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 11 and 12, Chef Sarah Glover brings her signature, land-driven approach to the flame, preparing locally sourced game, fresh-baked breads, and seasonal ingredients over open fire. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: Allen Stone
events.description:
Grammy-nominated artist Allen Stone delivers an unforgettable fusion of soul, funk, and feel-good energy. Allen Stone, a soul/R&B luminary, has enraptured global audiences with his electrifying performances and soulful melodies, embodying the essence of classic soul legends. 
His genre-defying style, blending funk, gospel, folk-rock, and soul, has garnered critical acclaim and a devoted following. Stone's journey to musical stardom commenced in Chewelah, Washington, where his upbringing immersed him in gospel music as a pastor's son. Relocating to Seattle, he tirelessly toured the West Coast in his '87 Buick, swiftly gaining acclaim for his dynamic live shows, often playing up to 200 dates annually alongside luminaries such as Stevie Wonder, Al Green, Dave Matthews Band, Erykah Badu, and Gary Clark Jr. Notably, Stone recently hit the road with Chris Stapleton, further solidifying his status as a tour de force in the music industry.
Alongside his prolific touring schedule, Stone has graced national television screens and mentored on ABC's American Idol, underscoring his esteemed position in the music realm. Stone continues to garner critical acclaim with his latest body of work, Mystery, featuring the title track in collaboration with PJ Morton. This release brings infectious rhythms and emotional depth, solidifying Stone’s place as a powerful voice in modern soul music. 
Opening support Wyatt Pike is an indie-folk songwriter known for crafting story-driven songs filled with vivid imagery, emotional honesty, and reflections on real-life experiences. He first gained attention with his debut EP, Brand New Boy, and most recently released the acoustic project Face The Weather. His music explores the journey of personal growth and self-discovery. As Alt Recess noted, his work represents "the next chapter—more assured, more grounded, but still brimming with the tender honesty that put him on the map." His upcoming 2026 releases will continue to build on that authentic and

events.title: Boney James
events.description:
Saxophonist, Producer and Composer Boney James’  New Album ‘Slow Burn’
slow burn: noun, informal
A feeling that grows with a slow but deliberate intensity.
For saxophonist and composer Boney James, these words perfectly describe the music he has  crafted for his new Concord Records album, Slow Burn, to be released on October 18, 2024.
“When I mentioned that phrase to people as a possible title, they said, “That also refers to your  whole career in a way” James says with a laugh. “This is my 19th record and I feel like I’m just  hitting my stride.”
Slow Burn is a new high point in a remarkable career that now spans more than three decades  and includes a long list of accolades; four GRAMMY nominations, three NAACP Image Award  nominations, a Soul Train Award for Best Jazz Album and four RIAA Gold Record certifications.  In 2009, Billboard named James one of the Top 3 Contemporary Jazz Artists of the decade. In  2024 year Boney became the first ever artist to score 20 number one singles on the Billboard Smooth Jazz chart.
But Boney James has never been one to look backwards and Slow Burn, which the artist  produced, marks several firsts. Beginning with the album’s opening track, ‘Arcadia.’ The very first notes on the album are the acoustic bass playing of the legendary Marcus Miller.  It’s one of the few times that Miller, known for his electric bass guitar work, has committed his  standup bass playing to a recording and the first time that James has incorporated an upright bass  into his own music
Next is James’ intriguing interpretation of the classic ‘Butterfly.’ Co-written by master  keyboardist Herbie Hancock and Bennie Maupin, the track features Marcus Miller again as well  as young lion Cory Henry’s inspired electric piano. “This beautiful melody got stuck in my head”  explains James. “I have never covered a tune by a legend like Herbie before and I hope we did  him justice.”
The title track, ‘Slow Burn,’ is one of four tunes on the album that James co-wrote an

UB40 Featuring Ali Campbell – Live in Concert
Get ready for a legendary night of reggae under the stars as UB40 featuring Ali Campbell brings their iconic sound to Concerts on the Slopes in Park City, where Music Meets the Mountains.
With Ali Campbell’s unmistakable voice leading the way, UB40 has delivered some of the most beloved songs of all time, including Red Red Wine, Can’t Help Falling in Love, Kingston Town, Here I Am (Come and Take Me), and Food for Thought. Their music has defined generations, blending reggae rhythms with pop sensibility and global appeal.
Set against the stunning backdrop of Canyons Village at Park City Mountain, Concerts on the Slopes offers a one-of-a-kind outdoor concert experience. Enjoy cool mountain temperatures, sweeping alpine views, and an energetic yet intimate setting with 5,000 seats and top-tier sound and production.
Make it a full Park City experience—arrive early, explore the village, and settle in for an unforgettable summer evening filled with timeless music, mountain air, and good vibes.

events.title: Better Than Ezra with Special Guests Vertical Horizon and TONIC
events.description:
Get ready for a night of iconic ’90s and early-2000s alternative rock as Better Than Ezra and Vertical Horizon take the stage together for an unforgettable live show.
Better Than Ezra, known for their chart-topping hits like “Good,” “Desperately Wanting,” and “King of New Orleans,” rose to prominence during the alternative rock boom of the 1990s. With their melodic hooks, thoughtful lyrics, and energetic live performances, the band has remained a fan favorite for decades.
Joining them is Vertical Horizon, the multi-platinum band behind massive radio hits “Everything You Want,” “You’re a God,” and “Best I Ever Had.” Their emotionally driven songwriting and polished rock sound helped define an era of modern alternative and continue to resonate with audiences today.
Together, these two beloved bands deliver a hit-packed night of nostalgia, sing-along anthems, and timeless alternative rock. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this show promises a high-energy, feel-good concert experience you won’t want to miss.
Music has an undeniable way of bringing people together, creating connections that run as deep as family. That bond is at the heart of Tonic, the GRAMMY® Award–nominated, multi-platinum rock trio made up of Emerson Hart (vocals, guitar), Jeff Russo (lead guitar), and Dan Lavery (bass).
Since forming in the mid-1990s, Tonic has built a lasting legacy through eloquent songwriting, lyrical honesty, and unforgettable melodies. Their debut album Lemon Parade went platinum and produced the iconic “If You Could Only See,” the #1 most-played rock song of 1997. The band went on to score six Top 10 singles, sell over 4 million records, and earn two GRAMMY® Award nominations, including for “Take Me As I Am.”
More than two decades later, Tonic’s music continues to resonate across generations. Fans—young and old—still sing every word, proving the songs are bigger than time and bigger than the band itself. After brief breaks and successful solo caree

events.title: Maoli
events.description:
Now breaking into the mainstream with a one-of-a-kind blend of island reggae and country soul, Hawaiian-born rising star Maoli is bringing his people—and his sound—to the world like never before.
The golden-voiced native of upcountry Maui has released nine independent albums, earned over half a billion global streams, and spent nearly 15 years touring and recording, becoming a major fixture across the Pacific Islands and beyond. Inspired by traditional Hawaiian music (the birthplace of the steel guitar), reggae, rock, and country legends like Dolly Parton, Willie Nelson, and George Strait, Maoli has built a signature style that connects Polynesia to the American West Coast—and all the way to Texas.
With a growing global fanbase, Maoli’s Hawaiian-country sound has fueled viral hits and powerful covers, including Brett Young’s “Mercy” (18+ million Spotify streams) and “In Case You Didn’t Know” (10+ million Spotify streams). Now teaming up with hit producers Dann and David Huff, Maoli continues bridging the gap between island soul and country tradition with his latest release, Last Sip of Summer (Island Vibe Edition)—out now.

events.title: Rick Springfield
events.description:
RICK SPRINGFIELD BIO: 
Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” "I've Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years.

events.title: Dylan Scott
events.description: ABOUT DYLAN SCOTT:Despite a multi-Platinum story and six No. 1 singles, including two fresh smashes in 2025 alone, Curb Records hitmaker Dylan Scott has always kept an underdog mindset. A rowdy roughneck with a romantic side – one whose modern edge was sharpened by timeless, emotional twang – the consummate family man broke out with the epic triple-Platinum devotion of “My Girl” in 2016 and followed up with scrappy, under-the-radar consistency. Chart-topping heart-forward anthems like “Hooked,” “Nobody” and “New Truck” all reached RIAA-certified status, but last year launched the crooner into the stratosphere. Posting back-to-back No. 1s with Gold-certified “Boys Back Home” (with Dylan Marlowe) and Platinum-certified “This Town’s Been Too Good to Us,” Scott wrapped 2025 as the third most-played country artist of 2025 and continues his hot streak with his current single, Platinum-certified “What He’ll Never Have,” which nears the Top 10 at Country radio and was honored by ASCAP as one of the most-performed country songs of 2025. With nearly 5 billion career streams, legions of fans and countless sold-out shows to his credit, his stardom has somehow grown in relative stealth. But with his third full album, Easy Does It, he’s not sweating. Sure of himself, his talent and his fans, Scott heads toward a new creative chapter with total confidence. Featuring 11 tracks, his signature vocal and vivid intensity take center stage on a set defined by personal truth – whether through heartbreak, romantic devotion or small-town dedication. The in-demand live act spent last year on his Country Till I Die Tour, headlining his biggest venues yet including his first-ever arenas and record-breaking attendance, and his Easy Does It Tour. He will continue headlining across the country on his Till I Can’t I Will Tour, launching this spring.

events.title: 80s Weekend with 38 Special
events.description:
38 Special – Live in Concert
Bring on the anthems. 38 Special delivers high-energy, Southern-fried rock that defined an era and still packs the house decades later. Known for blending catchy hooks with driving guitar riffs, the band has become a staple of American rock radio and live performance.
With iconic hits like “Hold On Loosely,” “Caught Up in You,” and “Rockin’ Into the Night,” 38 Special has built a legacy of feel-good, sing-along songs that cross generations. Their live shows are tight, powerful, and packed with the kind of rock-and-roll energy that turns a concert into a shared memory.
Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this is a show made for fans of classic rock, summer nights, and timeless music that still hits just right.
 
Fox Brothers Band
Get ready for an unforgettable night of high-energy entertainment with the Fox Brothers Band, one of Utah’s most sought-after live music acts. Blending the best of Country, Rock, and Pop, the band delivers a dynamic performance packed with crowd favorites, modern hits, and timeless classics that keep audiences singing and dancing all night long.
Known for their infectious energy, exceptional musicianship, and engaging stage presence, the Fox Brothers Band has built a reputation for creating memorable live experiences for audiences of all ages. The band's members have individually performed with or opened for nationally recognized artists including Chris Janson, Riley Green, Chase Rice, Ian Munsick, Brantley Gilbert, Neon Trees, and many others.

events.title: 80s Weekend with Sawyer Brown
events.description:
“We don’t have a lot of love songs. We have life songs.” – Mark Miller
For more than 40 years, Sawyer Brown has delivered high-energy, blue-collar country-rock that keeps audiences on their feet. From winning Star Search to sharing stages with legends like Kenny Rogers and Dolly Parton, they’ve built a career defined by nonstop touring and unforgettable live shows.
With 51 charted singles—including the #1 hit “Some Girls Do”—and over 6,000 performances worldwide, Sawyer Brown continues to bring the same grassroots spirit and electrifying presence that made them a household name.
Skylar Geer
Experience an evening of authentic country music with Utah singer-songwriter Skylar Geer, a talented performer known for his rich vocals, heartfelt songwriting, and energetic live shows. Blending traditional country roots with Southern rock influences, Skylar creates a sound that is both timeless and fresh, connecting with audiences through stories of life, love, and the American West.
After spending several years in Nashville honing his craft as a songwriter and performer, Skylar returned to Utah and has become a favorite on stages throughout the Mountain West. Backed by a talented band featuring fiddle, guitars, bass, and drums, his performances seamlessly mix original music with crowd-favorite country classics and modern hits, all delivered with his signature style.
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: 80s Weekend with Taylor Dayne and Exposé
events.description:
Taylor Dayne
Taylor Dayne is an American pop icon. With a career spanning three decades, Taylor’s Groundbreaking debut single “Tell It To My Heart” turned her into an overnight international star in 1987. She followed the smash hit with seventeen Top 20 singles over the course of her three-decade career, including Number Ones “Love Will Lead You Back”, “Prove Your Love”, and “I’ll Always Love You.”
Taylor has sold over 75 million albums and singles worldwide, earned three GRAMMY nominations, an American Music Award, multiple New York Music Awards, received New York Music Hall of Fame honors and ranked as the Number 18 Female Dance Artist of all time by Rolling Stone magazine. She is one of the very few artists in musical history to successfully crossover into almost every musical genre and chart with hit singles in Pop, Dance, R&B, Adult contemporary, and Rock.
Exposé
Get ready for a night of unforgettable 80s and 90s dance-pop when Exposé takes the stage. This legendary female vocal group from Miami helped define the freestyle and dance-pop sound of the late 1980s and early 1990s, delivering a string of iconic hits that still fill dance floors today.
With powerhouse vocals and timeless melodies, Exposé became the first group ever to score four Top 10 hits from their debut album, including classics like “Come Go With Me,” “Point of No Return,” “Let Me Be the One,” and their No. 1 smash hit “Seasons Change.”
Featuring the celebrated voices of Jeanette Jurado, Ann Curless, and Gioia Bruno, Exposé continues to tour and delight fans worldwide with their signature blend of high-energy dance anthems and heartfelt ballads.
From chart-topping radio hits to beloved freestyle club favorites, Exposé delivers a show packed with nostalgia, powerful vocals, and infectious rhythms that will have audiences singing along all night.
Join us for an electrifying evening celebrating one of the most successful girl groups of the 80s and a sound that defined a generation.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Mark Chesnutt
events.description:
Mark Chesnutt is one of country music’s most iconic voices, known for his unmistakable honky-tonk sound and a catalog of timeless hits including “Too Cold at Home,” “Brother Jukebox,” and “I Don’t Want to Miss a Thing.” A defining artist of the ’90s country era, Chesnutt earned 14 No. 1 singles, 23 Top 10 hits, over 12 million albums sold, and the CMA Horizon Award, with Billboard naming him one of the most-played country artists of the decade. Now energized and fully focused on performing, Chesnutt continues to deliver powerful, authentic live shows that celebrate classic country at its finest.
 
Love & Chaos
Bringing together the heart of Texas country, the soul of Americana, and the energy of country rock, Love & Chaos delivers a powerful live music experience that is equal parts authentic storytelling and high-octane entertainment. Known for their tight harmonies, driving guitars, and engaging stage presence, the band has built a loyal following throughout the Southwest and beyond.
Drawing inspiration from the rich traditions of Red Dirt and Texas country music, Love & Chaos combines thoughtful songwriting with a dynamic sound that ranges from heartfelt ballads to crowd-pleasing anthems. Their performances showcase both original music and fan-favorite covers, creating an atmosphere that keeps audiences singing, dancing, and coming back for more.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Lonestar
events.description:
Lonestar is a celebrated American country music group that earned global recognition with their timeless ballad “Amazed,” a crossover sensation that dominated the charts for 9 weeks at No. 1 on the country charts and 2 weeks at No. 1 on the pop charts. Known for blending heartfelt storytelling with a polished pop-country sound, the band — comprised of Dean Sams, Michael Britt, Keech Rainwater, and Drew Womack — has made a lasting impact on the genre.
With an impressive 22 top ten singles on the Billboard Hot Country Songs chart, including 10 that reached No. 1, Lonestar has built one of the most successful track records in modern country music. Their rise began in 1995 with the top-five single “Tequila Talking,” followed by chart-topping hits such as the rock-tinged “No News,” “Come Cryin’ to Me,” and “Everything’s Changed.”
The release of their quadruple-platinum 1999 album Lonely Grill firmly established their legacy, producing four No. 1 singles and showcasing the band’s signature ability to craft emotionally resonant, radio-friendly songs.
Lonestar’s achievements have earned them widespread acclaim, including multiple Academy of Country Music awards for New Vocal Group, Single of the Year, Song of the Year, and Humanitarian of the Year. They have also been honored by the Country Music Association with Vocal Group of the Year and the International Artist Achievement Award.
With more than 10.5 million records sold worldwide, Lonestar remains one of country music’s most enduring and influential bands, continuing to inspire fans with their heartfelt hits and remarkable musical legacy.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Walker Hayes
events.description:
Country superstar Walker Hayes is a singer-songwriter from Mobile, Alabama, known for blending country storytelling with pop and hip-hop-inspired production. His breakout smash “Fancy Like” topped country sales and streaming charts, spent over six months at No. 1 on Billboard’s Hot Country Songs chart, reached the Top 5 on the Billboard Hot 100, hit No. 1 at country radio, and earned Hayes his first GRAMMY nomination for Best Country Song. The viral hit was also featured in a nationwide Applebee’s commercial.
With additional fan favorites like “AA,” “Y’all Life,” and “U Gurl,” Hayes has become one of the top-selling and most-streamed artists in modern country music. His latest project, 17 Problems, is his most personal work to date—an introspective album shaped by resilience, faith, family, grief, and growth. Featuring both high-energy anthems and deeply emotional storytelling, the album highlights Hayes’ evolution as an artist and songwriter.
Known for his authenticity and engaging live performances, Hayes has appeared on Good Morning America, The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, Late Night with Seth Meyers, TODAY, and CBS Sunday Morning. Whether delivering chart-topping hits or stripped-down acoustic moments, Walker Hayes invites audiences into a powerful, honest, and unforgettable live experience.
Wildwood
Get ready for an evening of crowd-pleasing country music with Wildwood, one of Utah’s fastest-rising country acts. Led by powerhouse vocalists Chad Truman and Kate Miller, Wildwood delivers a dynamic blend of modern country hits, classic favorites, and original songs, all brought to life with rich harmonies, exceptional musicianship, and infectious energy.
Known for their engaging stage presence and ability to get audiences singing and dancing along, Wildwood has performed throughout the Mountain West, sharing stages with nationally recognized country artists and earning praise for their authentic sound and high-energy performances. events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Marc E. Bassy with MAX
events.description:
MAX and Marc E. Bassy come together for an unforgettable night of genre-blending pop, R&B, and high-energy live performance.
Multi-platinum artist MAX leads the night with his powerhouse vocals, infectious pop sound, and global hits like “Lights Down Low” and music from his latest album, LOVE IN STEREO, which have earned over 3 billion streams worldwide. Known for his dynamic stage presence and emotional delivery, MAX brings a live show full of energy, connection, and sing-along moments.
Special Guest - OpenerJoining him is Bay Area artist Marc E. Bassy, celebrated for his soulful vocals, genre-defying style, and hit “You & Me” (feat. G-Eazy). With a career built on authenticity and creative independence, Marc delivers a performance that feels both intimate and electrifying.Together, they bring powerful vocals, emotional storytelling, and undeniable chemistry to the stage—creating a dynamic live experience you won’t want to miss.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: I Love the 90's
events.description:
Get ready to throw it back to the ultimate decade of music, style, and unforgettable party anthems as I Love the 90s takes over Concerts on the Slopes on Friday, September 4!
Featuring legendary hitmakers Vanilla Ice, C&C Music Factory and Color Me Badd, this high-energy night will transport fans straight back to the golden era of pop, hip hop, and R&B. From the chart-topping smash “Ice Ice Baby” to the smooth harmonies of “I Wanna Sex You Up” and “All 4 Love,” this is the ultimate summer celebration of 90s nostalgia.
Bring your friends, sing along to the songs you grew up with, and dance under the stars at the beautiful Canyons Village in Park City.
DJ Proof will open the night to get the party started!
 
 
(VIP1) Nostalgia Package
 
Includes: 

One (1) Standing VIP Pit or Reserved Seat
One (1) VIP Laminate & Lanyard 
One (1) VIP Poster
One (1) VIP Exclusive Slap Bracelet 
One (1) VIP Exclusive Fanny Pack 
One (1) VIP Exclusive Keychain
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Howl-o-ween on Main Street
events.description: Costumed dog parade down Main Street. Locals dress up their pups (and themselves) for Halloween. Awards for best costume. Free, family-friendly, one of Park City's most photographed annual traditions.
events.location: Main Street, Main Street, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Park City 4th of July Parade & Fireworks
events.description: Annual Independence Day celebration. Morning parade down Main Street with floats, marching bands, and local groups. Evening fireworks over Park City. Family-friendly and free to attend.
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: Kimball Arts Festival 2026
events.description: Three-day juried fine arts and crafts festival on Main Street presented by the Kimball Art Center. 200+ artists, live music, food, and family activities. Park City's largest summer arts event. Confirm exact dates at kimballartcenter.org.
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: Miners Day Parade & Mucking and Drilling Competition
events.description: Park City's beloved Labor Day tradition honoring the town's mining heritage. Main Street parade followed by the historic mucking and drilling competition at City Park. Free, family friendly, classic Park City.
events.location: Main Street & City Park, Park City, UT

events.title: Autumn Aloft Hot Air Balloon Festival
events.description: Annual mid-September hot air balloon festival at the North 40 fields. Dawn launches both mornings, plus a Saturday night balloon glow. Free to watch. Bring coffee — launches are early.
events.location: North 40 Fields, Park City, UT

events.title: Electric Parade & Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Park City's holiday season kickoff. Electric Parade down Main Street featuring lit floats and the historic tree lighting at Miners Park. Hot cocoa, carolers, Santa. Free, family-friendly. Confirm exact date at visitparkcity.com — typically the first Saturday of December.
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: Community Trail Run! Speedgoat Training!
events.description:
**Speedgoat Training Run + Demo**

Getting ready for Speedgoat? Join us for a community training run at the Armstrong Trailhead featuring demo shoes from HOKA, foot care support from Sidas, and recovery and performance services from FIKA Wellness.

We'll be kicking off the run at **8:00 AM**, but arrive as early as **7:30 AM** to get set up with a pair of **HOKA Speedgoat 7 demo shoes**, connect with the team from **Sidas**for foot care solutions, and take advantage of pre-run services from **FIKA Wellness**.

**We will be gathering in the First Time Lift parking lot at Park City Mountain Resort!**

What to Expect

👟 **HOKA Speedgoat 7 Demo**

Test the latest Speedgoat 7 on the trails

Demo shoes available before the run

🦶 **Sidas Foot Care**

Expert foot care solutions and fitting advice

Learn how the right support can keep your feet comfortable and happy through long training days

💉 **FIKA Wellness**

Free B12 shots

Discounted NAD+ shots

Pre-run mini IVs available

Post-run recovery IVs available

The Run

This will be a self-guided community run with a suggested route and plenty of flexibility to choose your own adventure.

Our recommended route follows **Armstrong Trail to Pinecone Ridge**, totaling approximately **18 miles** with plenty of climbing—an excellent training day for runners preparing for Speedgoat.

Looking for fewer miles? No problem. There are several shorter route options from the trailhead, making this event accessible to a wide range of runners and training goals.

Who Should Come?

Whether you're training for Speedgoat, building fitness for another race, or simply looking for a great morning on the trails with the community, we'd love to have you join us.

All paces are welcome!...
events.location: First Time Lift, Park City, UT

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Jack Johnson: SURFILMUSIC
events.description:
"SURFILMUSIC" Screens Friday Jun. 12 - Saturday Jun. 13 at 7:00 PM & Sunday Jun. 14 at 6:00 PM

SURFILMUSIC, a documentary chronicling Johnson's evolution from surfer to filmmaker to world-renowned musician, weaves through the making of his iconic surf films "Thicker Than Water" \(1999\) and "The September Sessions" \(2000\). These classic films revealed Johnson's artistic point of view through his lens and guitar, ultimately launching his music career.

The new film celebrates the lifelong friendships and ocean-driven community that shaped Johnson's path, and features many of the surfers who appeared in the original films, including Kelly Slater, Rob Machado, and the Malloy Brothers. It also captures the shared spirit of exploration and creativity that continues to inspire his music and environmentalism today.

Beer and wine available for purchase.

Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: The Great Weed Roundup with Summit Land Conservancy
events.description:
Summit Land Conservancy and Summit County are looking for motivated volunteers to help us uproot noxious weeds!

With spring fully in swing, join us for our next Stewardship Saturday at Miss Billie’s Preserve (Summit Community Gardens)! We will be pulling invasive dyer's woad, thistle, and white top while they are in bloom and before they can release their seeds. These are all aggressive, introduced plants that grow rapidly and create dense thickets which crowd out our native grasses and wildflowers.

When we remove these weeds, we help to restore our local grassland ecosystem and ensure that native plants can thrive and support the wildlife that depends on them.This project will require using gardening tools and manual pulling to stop the spread of these weeds in their tracks. Participants should bring clothing suitable for a full day outside (long pants, long sleeves, sun protection, warm layers, hat, sunglasses, and sturdy shoes), gardening gloves if you have them, along with a water bottle and snacks.

Summit Land Conservancy will bring coffee, bagels and cold refreshments to keep you caffeinated, fueled and hydrated while you work!...
events.location: Miss Billie's Preserve, 4056 Shadow Mountain Drive, Park City, UT 84098, USA

events.title: Moose on the Loose Kids Trail Race
events.description:
Join us for **Moose on the Loose 2026,** a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit.

The 2026 race dates are **June 13**, **July 18**, **August 15**, and **September 12**, with all races beginning at **9:00 AM**. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer.

Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....

events.title: An Evening with Dr. N. T. Wright
events.description:
2000 years of questions — 1 night of answers.

The questions on the table are the ones keeping thoughtful leaders up at 2 am — a groaning creation, a divided church, and a world that’s lost its patience for easy answers. They’re the ones surfacing in your sermons, your elder meetings, and your quiet moments of doubt. 

Dr. N.T. Wright has spent a lifetime carefully working through difficult questions about faith. Join us for an evening of exploring difficult questions together. 

5:00 – Appetizers & Mingling

5:30 – Presentation by N.T. Wright

6:30 – Q&A with N.T. Wright

7:00 – Continued Conversations \(optional\) 

$15 secures your seat. RSVP is required. 

Don’t let finances be a barrier — reach out to Eli@ccofpc.org or Evelyn@ccofpc.org if you’d like a scholarship....

events.title: Kirtan in Park City | Music & Mantras by Spirit Soul
events.description: A joyful immersion in music, mantras, and dance Let Your Heart Soar Kirtan is a joyous form of meditation that combines music with hearing and singing sacred mantras. Singing these powerful transcendental sounds together frees our mind of stress and anxiety and awakens a deep sense of peace, connection, and joy. The Kirtan Experience is the work of a growing collective of meditators, musicians, artists and friends from around the world who have been deeply touched by this practice and feel inspired to share it with others. These events are an offering from our hearts, and we keep the events free so that anyone can attend. Our goal is to rekindle joy, peace and true inner freedom in a world deeply in need of healing. What You'll Experience • Live, immersive musical meditation with vibrant visuals, guided by lifelong kirtan artists and musicians. • Heartfelt singing of simple, powerful mantras that are easy to follow. No experience needed. • A comfortable, welcoming space where you can sing, dance, or simply kick back and listen. • A dynamic experience that flows between meditative moments and uplifting, energetic peaks. • A chance to unplug, reset, and feel alive. We welcome you to join us. Meditation Meets Celebration This is not a performance. It’s an enlivening experience created and shared by everyone who attends. A Living Tradition The gift we are sharing has been passed down with great care for over 500 years. Lovingly preserved in the lineage of Sri Krishna Chaitanya, the yoga sound is as spiritually potent and purifying today as it was centuries ago. Everyone Is Welcome Whether you're arriving with a light heart or a heavy burden, you are welcome here. In Kirtan, people come together across differences. It requires no agreement, belief, or explanation. Just come as you are and let the transcendental sound carry you into another world. Spring 2026 Tour This spring, Spirit Soul is bringing The Kirtan Experience to: • Lake Tahoe, NV • Sacramento, CA • Nevada ...
events.location: Park City Community Church (United Methodist), Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Dig Day June 16th
events.description: Thanks for your interest in volunteering to help us build trails! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. Mountain Trails Foundation will be completing two new sections of directional, downhill flow trail on PCMR. To accomplish this, we will be utilizing volunteers to complete some of the finish work. Work anticipated will be the removal of debris from trail, smoothing of tread, compaction of features, and the removal of vegetation from the trail corridor. These events are special, as they are in the evening and generally end around sunset and, as everyone knows, that is a wonderful time to be on the mountain.  We'll meet at the First Time Parking Lot \(https://maps.app.goo.gl/SE1bcWNVw4sH17vZA\) at 5:00pm, and shuttles to the worksite will leave at 5:15 sharp! We'll return to the parking lot around 8pm.  We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. Minors must be accompanied by a guardian, and we ask for a 5:1 ratio of minors to adults.  Timeframe: 5pm Meet at the trailhead 5:15pm Shuttles leave for worksite 8:00 Return to parking lot Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description:
Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth.

Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.title: Park City Community Foundation's Summer Solstice Celebration
events.description:
Join Park City Community Foundation as we celebrate summer and show support for our local community! Start your weekend with drinks, food, live music, face painting, games, local resources for parents & kids, and fun for all ages! Bring your friends and get connected at this free, community-wide celebration and fair. 

Be sure to stop by our tables to learn about Park City Community Foundation and our initiatives including Early Childhood Alliance, Youth United, Zero Food Waste, Women's Giving Fund, and Community Pass Club - and how you can contribute and get involved.  You will also have a chance to bid on Cabriolet Cabins donated by CVMA and Vail Resorts Epic Promise Foundation and Park City Mountain, with funds supporting Park City Community Foundation.

Parking is available in the upper village lots.

Thank you **Vail Resorts Epic Promise Foundation and Park City Mountain** for sponsoring this event....

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Tuner
events.description: Leo Woodall stars as Niki, a gifted young piano tuner whose heightened sense of hearing draws the attention of criminals, who see his talents as useful for opening safes as for tuning Steinways. With his once-promising musical career over, Niki works across New York with his mentor Harry Horowitz \(Dustin Hoffman\), encountering a range of characters, including composition student Ruthie \(Havana Rose Liu\), with whom he forges an unexpected connection. Niki's safecracking work threatens his budding romance with Ruthie and pulls him into increasingly dangerous territory. The story blends romance, drama, and the taut suspense of a heist thriller. events.title: Park City Film Presents: Tuner
events.description: Leo Woodall stars as Niki, a gifted young piano tuner whose heightened sense of hearing draws the attention of criminals, who see his talents as useful for opening safes as for tuning Steinways. With his once-promising musical career over, Niki works across New York with his mentor Harry Horowitz \(Dustin Hoffman\), encountering a range of characters, including composition student Ruthie \(Havana Rose Liu\), with whom he forges an unexpected connection. Niki's safecracking work threatens his budding romance with Ruthie and pulls him into increasingly dangerous territory. The story blends romance, drama, and the taut suspense of a heist thriller. Part of the 2026 Sundance Film Festival Spotlight section....

events.title: Father's Day with Grand Hyatt Deer Valley
events.description:
Family celebration is kicking into high gear just in time for Father’s Day at the mountain favorite Remington Hall. On June 21st at 5:00 p.m., Grand Hyatt Deer Valley’s on-property restaurant will serve the East Village’s most thoughtfully curated multi-course prix-fixe menu. 

Designed by the culinary team to highlight the season’s finest ingredients, course highlights include a smoked trout rillettes appetizer, filet mignon, and seared scallop entrées, with desserts ranging from cinnamon crème brûlée to bourbon bread pudding. Each course showcases rich flavors, with an optional whiskey pairing by Alpine Distilling — an exceptional way to honor Dad....

Presenters include:

\- Rich Sonntag, retired developer & housing authority commissioner (moderator)

\- Jess Kirby, director of Summit County Lands & Natural Resources

\- Sarah Tabak, programs specialist for Summit Land Conservancy

\- Luke Cartin, director of Lands & Sustainability for Park City Municipal

Hosted by the Summit County Housing Authority, the speaker series tackles hard questions facing community development — everything from transportation to open space to the role of government and local businesses....

events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description:
A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level.

Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath.

\*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged

\* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing.

Some benefits of breathwork include:

- Connect to your creativity

- Increase Self Love and appreciation

- Stress relief

- Increased Self Awareness

- Ease symptoms of Depression

- Ease symptoms of Anxiety

- Peace of mind

- Open the heart

- Increased Intuition

- Relaxation

- Release trauma and fear

- Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings

10-Class Pass Rate: $11

Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description:
Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth.

Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

**Mountain Mediation Y2K Trivia Night at O'Shucks!**

At this **family-friendly event**, grab your frosted lip gloss, low-rise jeans, and flip phones!! It’s time for a Y2K Trivia Night fundraiser benefiting Mountain Mediation and co-hosted with O'Shucks Bar & Grill!

Join us for a fun night of early-2000s themed trivia, food, prizes, and community at O'Shucks in Park City!!

**Event Details**

Date: Wednesday, June 24

Time: 6:00 PM – 9:00 PM

Trivia Begins: 7:00 PM

Location: O'Shucks Bar & Grill 8178 Gorgoza Pines Rd a, Park City, UT 84098

**Trivia Format**

4 rounds of Y2K-themed trivia

Teams of up to 4 players

Prizes for winning teams and round winners

Fun Y2K-themed giveaways throughout the night

**Tickets**

**Team Registration (Up to 4 Players) – $100**

Includes:

Entry for up to 4 players

Two complimentary appetizers for your team

**Individual Player – $30**

Register solo and we’ll place you on a team.

**Spectator / Community Support Ticket – $10**

Come hang out, support Mountain Mediation, and enjoy the atmosphere without participating in trivia.

**All ages are welcome.**

**About Mountain Mediation**

*Mountain Mediation works to strengthen communities through conflict resolution, housing stability support, and accessible mediation services throughout Utah.*

*All proceeds from this event support Mountain Mediation’s community programs and services.*...

events.title: Summit County Dems Pizza and Fundraising
events.description:
**Wednesday, June 24**

**6:00 to 8:00 PM**

**The Red Banjo**

**322 Main Street, Park City**

*Featuring Special Guest Brian King, * *Chair of the Utah Democratic Party*

We're launching this fundraising campaign with a fun, informal evening featuring community conversation, food, and an opportunity to help build a stronger, more effective local political organization rooted in competence, integrity, and community-focused leadership.

Tickets are $100 and include buffet pizza and a drink ticket. We also offer a no-questions-asked sliding scale because we believe civic participation should be accessible to everyone.

Your support will directly fund voter outreach efforts here in Summit County at a time when outside political spending and coordinated campaigning are expected to play a larger role in local races.

*If you cannot attend, we hope you will still consider making a contribution in support of this work!*...

Learn to mix, shake, and enjoy your own handcrafted, seaso nally inspired cocktails! Upon arrival, we will greet you with a glass of sparkling rosé or brut. Sit back and sip while your instructor explains the bartender's tools of the trade and how to use them. Then, get your cameraphone ready, as you will be expertly guided through two shaken-or-stirred recipes to ensure a delicious final product.

Perfect for:

  \* Bachelor/ Bachelorette Parties

  \* Birthday & Anniversary Celebrations

  \* Honeymoons

  \* Team-Building Events and Corporate Retreats

Choose your theme!

  \* Seasonally-Inspired/Holidays

  \* Farm-to-Table Margaritas or Smashes

  \* Old Fashioned versus Manhattan

  \* Sparkling Wine Cocktails

  \* New Orleans Favorites

  \* NA and Mocktail Classes

Must be 21 to enter Old Town Cellars. No guests under 21 allowed....

events.description:
Join us for a wildflower walk on the 910 Ranch, led by Lands & Natural Resources staff and volunteers.

What to Expect:

This event involves a walk of approximately 2-3 miles. Please dress appropriately for the conditions—wear sturdy footwear, sun-protective clothing, hats, and sunscreen. Don’t forget to bring water. We recommend bringing layers to stay comfortable! A few pairs of binoculars will be available to borrow.

Youth Participation:

Participants under the age of 18 are welcome to attend L&NR events but must be accompanied and supervised by a parent or legal guardian at all times. Parents or guardians should reserve additional tickets for any participants who are under the age of 18....

events.title: Thriving Land Thursdays: Weed Wrangling on the 910 Ranch
events.description:
Join us at the 910 Ranch for a hands-on day of stewardship and community connection! We'll work together to restore the riparian corridor by removing noxious and invasive weeds that crowd out native species and degrade wildlife habitat.

What to Bring & Wear: Wear long pants, long sleeves, and sturdy closed-toe shoes or boots. Pack sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen), water and snacks, and layers in case of changing weather. Please bring sunglasses to act as protective eyewear! Participants may want bring their own thick, sturdy gloves and clippers. Summit County will have equipment available to borrow.

Safety Notice: Participation in weed removal can be hazardous. Hazards may include cuts, punctures, scrapes, tripping, heavy lifting, sharp edges, and exposure to rusted metal or other environmental risks. By signing up and attending this activity, you acknowledge these risks, accept full responsibility for your participation, and agree to the terms of the waiver. Your safety is our priority—please work carefully, follow instructions, and look out for one another.

This is a great opportunity to roll up your sleeves, meet like-minded people, and directly contribute to preserving and enhancing one of our community’s treasured open spaces....
events.location: 910 Ranch, 10063 North East Canyon Road, Snyderville, UT 84098, USA

events.title: nature relief prints
events.description: nature relief prints Thursday, June 25th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required Join us for a hands-on nature relief printing workshop where participants will create bold, large-scale prints using a tree stump as the printing surface. Each student will produce a striking tree print \(approximately 25 x 40 inches\) and explore additional smaller prints using plants and flowers. This workshop introduces the fundamentals of relief printing, including inking techniques, proper ink coverage, and printmaking processes. Participants will gain experience working with natural textures and forms while creating one-of-a-kind prints to take home. Please wear clothing that can get dirty, as printing ink may stain and bring any plants from home you want to print. Rebecca Pletsch is a Utah-based painter and mixed media artist whose work explores themes of memory, impermanence, and perception through painting and collage. With over twenty years of experience, her practice merges traditional techniques with experimental processes, creating layered works that evoke atmosphere, fragmentation, and emotional resonance. Her work has received numerous awards and honors and has been exhibited in major museums and galleries throughout Utah, including the Utah Museum of Contemporary Art, Ogden Contemporary Arts, the Kimball Art Center, the Rio Gallery, and the Springville Museum of Art. In addition to her studio practice, she is deeply engaged in arts education and is passionate about helping others explore and develop their creative voice. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT

events.title: "Horizons" New Paintings by Luke Anderson
events.description:
In his latest body of work, Luke Anderson explores the enduring character of the American West through a contemporary lens. Balancing sweeping landscapes with intimate moments of ranch life, Anderson's paintings capture the rugged beauty, quiet resilience, and timeless spirit of the places and people that define the region. Rich in atmosphere and authenticity, these works invite viewers to experience the West not as a relic of the past, but as a living and evolving landscape.

Join us for the opening reception on **Friday, June 26, 6-8 pm**. Guests will meet the artist and enjoy a live music and refreshments....

events.title: Cory Mon
events.description:
..music, my blessing, my curse, its in me and it must come out i can't help it, i must play, write, compose and perform. Without i am incomplete, while with call me misfit in this world of the incorporated. I am a man confused and divided thus in recourse i am often brightened and enlightened. I emphatically give way to both light and dark, i live in pleasure and pain. my name: cory.

Singer/songwriter Cory Mon has a distinctly unique, albeit pleasant voice in the world of music. “Happy \(sometimes sad\), gritty, funky, folksy, bluesy, rocky music… I guess”… he says when asked the dreaded, “So what type of music do you play?” Assuming the life of a musician, Cory has thrived as well as endured through the many ups and downs that have come throughout his well tenured 20\+ year music career. Cory has released 60\+ original songs and toured with and/or supported a handful of his favorite artists: JJ Grey & Mofro, Sean Hayes, The Avett Brothers, Patterson Hood etc. 

www.corymon.com...
events.location: Kimball Art Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Third Act
events.description: Generations of artists call Robert A. Nakamura “The Godfather of Asian American film,” but his son, Tad, calls him Dad. As the filmmaking son of a filmmaking legend, Tad uses the lessons his dad taught him to decipher the legacy of an aging man who was a child survivor of the WWII incarceration of Japanese Americans, a successful photographer who gave it up to tell his own story, an activist at the dawn of a social movement—and a father whose struggles have won his son freedoms that eluded Japanese Americans of his generation. As Parkinson’s Disease clouds his memory, Tad sets out to retrieve his story—and in the process discovers his own. The two have made films together, with Robert always by Tad’s side. “Third Act” is most likely the last. 2025 Sundance Film Festival premiere. Post-Film Q&A with director Tadashi Nakamura and others TBA.  “A richly layered examination of the generational effects of segregation, grief, parenthood, and art’s ability to help us make sense of it all.” — Courtney Small, POV Magazine Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) – a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence...
events.location: Park City Film, Park City, UT

events.title: Plein Air Painting: The Living Landscape
events.description:
Join painter Johnny DeFeo for three immersive days of plein air exploration, where landscape becomes both subject and teacher. Rather than a traditional “paint-along,” instruction is intentionally intimate and responsive, with frequent mini-critiques and one-on-one guidance. With short lectures and demonstrations all on location, develop your own visual language and body of work.

Participants should bring their own supplies and have prior painting experience. This workshop is designed for artists ready to deepen their relationship with landscape and push their work beyond observation into expression....
events.location: Kimball Art Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Give Me the Ball
events.description: World champion tennis trailblazer Billie Jean King has had a game-changing impact on culture and sports. Rare archive and candid interviews with Billie Jean and those closest to her reveal how one woman put changing the world ahead of saving herself. The story of professional women’s sports could not be told without Billie Jean King. Her status is so iconic, some might lose sight of the real human being — her struggles, her flaws, and her all-consuming, competitive drive. In fighting for equity for herself and others, King was uncompromising, to the point of subordinating her personal well-being by hiding her sexual orientation and eating disorders. Directors Liz Garbus and Elizabeth Wolff present Billie Jean King in full, in her own words, and with archival footage that brings the dark times and indelible victories to life again. With refreshing honesty and the hard-won perspective of having lived through it all, “Give Me the Ball!” is an electric portrait of one of the greatest of all time. 2026 Sundance Film Festival premiere.  Post-film panel discussion TBA. Presented with Summit Pride Foundation. Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) – a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence...
events.location: Park City Film, Park City, UT

events.title: "Tucked In" New Paintings by Colby Sanford
events.description:
*Tucked In* is a new collection of paintings by Colby Sanford that celebrates the quiet beauty of home, family, and the landscapes that shape our daily lives. Through luminous light, rich color, and thoughtful composition, Sanford captures moments of stillness and belonging, inviting viewers to reflect on the places and relationships that ground us. These works offer a contemporary perspective on the enduring comforts of connection, memory, and place.

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description:
Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. 

No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). 

The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description:
Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. 

Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....
events.location: Glenwood Cemetery, Park City, UT

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description:
Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more.

 

Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin.

 

In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
