events.title: Longevity, Simplified: An Expert-Led Conversation
events.description:
Black Rock Mountain Resort’s Onyx Club is partnering with local wellness experts for its June Speaker Series event, *Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health*, to focus on the practical application of long-term health optimization. The enlightening event, which is free and open to the community, will take place on Wednesday, June 24, from 5:30 to 7:00 p.m. at Black Rock Mountain Resort in Heber City, Utah.

The event brings together two respected, local leaders in longevity, health, and performance: Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, co-founder of The Smart Fit Method.

Designed to cut through the noise surrounding wellness trends and longevity advice, the discussion will focus on the four pillars of Longevity — sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress management — and how individuals can apply evidence-based strategies to improve daily performance, vitality, and long-term health outcomes. Participants are encouraged to attend with questions for the Q&A to follow the brief, informative presentations.

Attendees will gain insight into:

The lifestyle factors that have the greatest impact on longevity and overall health

How advanced diagnostics such as lab testing, body composition analysis, and VO₂ max assessments can personalize wellness strategies

Which health metrics matter most for optimizing performance and extending health span

Practical ways to implement sustainable habits within demanding, real-world schedules

Darnbrough will share the story behind The Smart Fit Method, a science-driven training system developed through his own experience overcoming severe injuries and rebuilding strength after a professional skiing career. The approach, embraced by everyone from elite athletes looking to boost their performance to seniors prioritizing their healthspan, emphasizes efficient, sustainable training designed to improve resilience, recovery, and long-term physical performance.

Dr. Duerler brings ...

events.title: Utah Symphony
events.description: We're bringing a symphonic celebration of America's 250th birthday to Utah's Little Switzerland! Enjoy a performance by our musicians against green backdrops and crisp alpine air, right in the heart of Midway's Town Square. The program has a patriotic flair, featuring favorite classical works, Broadway selections, American folk music, and more, including: Works from celebrated American composers like Aaron Copland and Leroy Anderson, including John Williams' Liberty Fanfare. Western-inspired pieces \(perfect for iconic Western vistas\)-Elie Siegmeister's Western Suite and Aaron Copland's "Hoe-Down" from Rodeo. Western Suite was first recorded with the Utah Symphony under Maurice Abravanel in 1971. Iconic classical works like the beautiful Adagietto from Mahler's Symphony No. 5, a Maurice Abravanel favorite and first recorded by the Utah Symphony in 1975. Performances by violinist Aubree Oliverson, a Utah native and recent Masterworks soloist for the Utah Symphony. Soprano Amy Owens \(former Utah Opera Resident Artist\) performs "Glitter and Be Gay" from Bernstein's Candide, as well as "Quando m'en vo" from Puccini's La bohème and Ricky Ian Gordon's "Simple Child" from The Grapes of Wrath. These pieces honor Utah's world-class arts legacy: La bohème was Utah Opera's first production in 1978 and The Grapes of Wrath was co-commissioned and premiered in 2007....
events.location: Midway Town Hall, Midway, UT

events.title: Rigby Summer
events.description:
Formerly touring as summerland, Oklahoma-based songwriter, Rigby Summer, weaves an Americana soundscape that reflects the road she’s traveled, from her Kansas City jazz upbringing to the beach pop of her former home in southern California to the red dirt roots of her current home in Stillwater, Oklahoma. While her influences range from Mary Chapin Carpenter to The Frames, Sia Furler to Johnny Cash, her sound often draws comparisons to Ingrid Michaelson and Peggy Lee—a little modern, a little old-fashioned, always earnest.

In 2017, after a five-year hiatus from performing music, she founded the Monday@Modella songwriter pop-up and the ALL MUSIC STARTS LOCAL initiative and started sharpening her musical teeth, slowly carving a niche for herself in the Oklahoma scene. Since then, she has had the privilege of sharing the stage with and learning from a myriad of local and touring artists, including Bill Scorzari, Belle Plaine, Blake Berglund, Heather Styka and many more. She was also given honorable mention in the 2017 Woody Guthrie Festival Songwriting Contest and was a semi-finalist in the 2019 Wyoming Singer-Songwriter Contest, in which she was voted as the audience choice winner for her round.

Hher first full-length album “Geography” was released in October 21 and broke the top ten on the NACC Folk Chart in November 2021....

events.title: Alicia Stockman Band
events.description: Alicia Stockman is a Utah-based folk-meets-Americana singer-songwriter whose music pulls back the veil to reveal everyday vulnerability. Her songs are written like intimate moments, drawing listeners into a relatable emotional journey, guided along by her whisky-dipped soulful vocals. Influenced by the vivid storytelling of powerhouse songwriters like Patty Griffin, Indigo Girls, and Jewel, she finds her home in the folk and Americana scene.
events.location: Heber Market on Main, Heber City, UT

Every Saturday from 7:00-8:30 PM, Main Street Park comes alive with live music, good food, and a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere as the sun sets over downtown. The series features a diverse lineup of local musicians, highlighting the depth of talent in the Heber Valley and surrounding communities. events.title: Ragnar Trail Soldier Hollow 2026
events.description: Ragnar Trail Soldier Hollow is happening August 28-29, 2026! Join us this summer for our return to the great state of Utah in our inaugural Solider Hollow trail relay! Run the trails at Soldier Hollow where Olympic history was made and future champions will compete. Smooth singletrack, rolling doubletrack, and a rugged ridgeline challenge your endurance while rewarding every step. Take in sweeping views of Heber Valley, Soldier Hollow Golf Course, Mount Timpanogos, and Deer Creek Reservoir by day, then experience the starry Wasatch skies by night! Register here: https://runragnar.com/pages/trail-solider-hollow...
events.location: Soldier Hollow Nordic Center, Charleston, UT
events.address: Wilderness Access Outfitters, Olympic Dr, Midway

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description:
Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date.

A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere.

Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: UT Concealed Firearm Permit Class at Sportsman's Warehouse HEBER CITY, UT
events.description: This 4-hour combined Utah Concealed Firearms Permit \(CFP\) and Arizona Concealed Weapons Permit \(CWP\) class is a comprehensive training course designed to educate individuals on the laws and regulations surrounding the carrying of concealed firearms. The class covers essential topics such as the use of force, firearms safety, and the proper handling and storage of firearms, as well as the specific laws and requirements for obtaining a permit in Arizona and Utah. DATES and TIMES are subject to change, please view our website for the most up to date and accurate class info. https://www.legalheat.com/classes/in-person/ut-concealed-firearm-permit-class-at-sportsmans-warehouse-heber-city-ut/?utm\_source=facebook&utm\_medium=event...
events.location: Heber City, UT

events.title: Ride TBM 309 in Heber City, UT
events.description: Ride the historic TBM Avenger \#309 when we join our friends at the CAF Utah Wing for their annual Planes and Horsepower Show. TBM 309 is the only aircraft on Colorado's register of historic places.  It flew over Queen Elizabeth's Coronation and appeared in Close Encounters of the Third Kind.  Book a flight with her today at the ticket link in this event!

events.title: A Dog's Way Home (Kamas)
events.description: As a puppy, Bella finds her way into the arms of Lucas, a young man who gives her a good home. When Bella becomes separated from Lucas, she soon finds herself on an epic 400-mile journey to reunite with her beloved owner. Along the way, the lost but spirited dog touches the lives of an orphaned mountain lion, a down-on-his-luck veteran and some friendly strangers who happen to cross her path. Based on the book of the same name by W. Bruce Cameron.  Join us under the stars in Kamas for a free film screening with free food trucks! Presented with Spanish Subtitles. Event Details: Outdoor film screening at the Kamas City Park \(505 S Main St, Kamas, UT 84036\) Free Food Trucks on site from 5-7pm, movie at 7pm \(with Spanish subtitles\) Blankets and lawn chairs welcome In partnership with the Kamas Valley Community Foundation, we are accepting donations of non-perishable, unexpired food items to benefit the Kamas and Oakley Food Pantries. Presented in partnership with Summit County Health...
events.location: Kamas, UT

events.title: Table for Twelve, The Market in Midway, Wine & Cheese, June 11th, 6pm
events.description:
**Table for Twelve, Cheese & Wine Tasting at The Market in Midway – Thursday, June 11th, 6 pm**

**Table for Twelve is back!** Now in Midway. ***Thursday , June 11th, 6 pm,*** this month **“A French Summer”**.

Come taste through four to five specialty cheeses from France paired with four five hand selected French wines.

 Your host, Pamela Wood, is a Licensed State Educator, Certified Sommelier, Specialist of Wine & Certified Wine Educator. Come learn about the history behind the wine and cheese and gain some new insights on pairings and easy serving tips!

A small group setting sets the stage to enjoy and share this experiences with friends. Must be 21+, Cost is $65.

Reservations required, space is limited – All wines are available from the local DABS store and cheeses are available at the Market!

The Market Midway, 42 W Main St., Midway, UT...
events.location: The Market in Midway, Midway, UT

events.title: Music Elevated State Tour
events.description: We’re bringing a symphonic celebration of America’s 250th birthday to Utah’s Little Switzerland! Enjoy a performance by our musicians against green backdrops and crisp alpine air, right in the heart of Midway’s Town Square. The program has a patriotic flair, featuring favorite classical works, Broadway selections, American folk music, and more, including: - Works from celebrated American composers like Aaron Copland and Leroy Anderson, including John Williams’ Liberty Fanfare. Western-inspired pieces \(perfect for iconic Western vistas\)—Elie Siegmeister’s Western Suite and Aaron Copland’s “Hoe-Down” from Rodeo. Western Suite was first recorded with the Utah Symphony under Maurice Abravanel in 1971. - Iconic classical works like the beautiful Adagietto from Mahler’s Symphony No. 5, a Maurice Abravanel favorite and first recorded by the Utah Symphony in 1975. - Performances by violinist Aubree Oliverson, a Utah native and recent Masterworks soloist for the Utah Symphony. - Soprano Amy Owens \(former Utah Opera Resident Artist\) performs “Glitter and Be Gay” from Bernstein’s Candide, as well as “Quando m’en vo” from Puccini’s La bohème and Ricky Ian Gordon’s “Simple Child” from The Grapes of Wrath. These pieces honor Utah’s world-class arts legacy: La bohème was Utah Opera’s first production in 1978 and The Grapes of Wrath was co-commissioned and premiered in 2007...
events.location: Midway Music on the Square, Midway, UT

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description:
Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date.

A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere.

Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description:
Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting.

The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!”

Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. 

Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm.

Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds."

GorgeousGourds.net...

events.title: Eric Heideman
events.description:
Eric Heideman is a 27-year-old blues musician living in Austin, Texas, from Salt Lake City, Utah.

Heideman was personally requested to open shows for ZZ Top at Eccles Theatre and Walter Trout and Mike Zito at The State Room. He was a featured performer at the Utah Blues Fest in 2022, 2023, & 2024. 

He was a featured performer at SXSW 2025 and the 2025 Durango Blues Train.

He recorded his latest album, “Third Degree Gravity” with Grammy Nominated Victor Wainwright and International Blues Challenge winner JW-Jones.

events.title: Queen Nation - Tribute to the Music of Queen at  Forum Fest At Canyons VIllage
events.description: Queen Nation, a tribute to Queen, was formed in 2004. Queen Nation consists of: Gregory Finsley on vocals & keyboards as Freddie Mercury, Mike McManus on guitar as Brian May, Pete Burke on drums as Roger Taylor, and Parker Combs on bass as John Deacon. The mission of the show is to carry on the musical torch and pay homage to the golden age of vintage Queen concerts. Queen Nation’s live 90-minute production of Queen’s greatest hits preserves the image, sound, and stage persona of vintage Queen. Audiences are amazed at the accuracy of Queen Nation’s live retrospective journey through such Queen songs as We Will Rock You – Bohemian Rhapsody – We Are The Champions – Crazy Little Thing Called Love – You’re My Best Friend – Another One Bites The Dust – Under Pressure and many more. They have performed in front of thousands of people at casinos, fairs, festivals and private affairs. For more info visit www.queennation.com....
events.location: Forum Fest At Canyons VIllage, 4000 Canyon Resort Drive, Park City

events.title: Alicia Stockman
events.description: Alicia Stockman is a Utah-based folk-meets-Americana singer-songwriter whose music pulls back the veil to reveal everyday vulnerability. Her songs are written like intimate moments, drawing listeners into a relatable emotional journey, guided along by her whisky-dipped soulful vocals. Influenced by the vivid storytelling of powerhouse songwriters like Patty Griffin, Indigo Girls, and Jewel, she finds her home in the folk and Americana scene.

events.title: The Last Light
events.description:
Join us for an incredible night of good vibes and heavy grooves as we bring our unique style of high desert reggae, rock, and groove music to Park City!

The Last Light is a reggae based groove collective composed of 7 artists who call the Southern Utah Desert home. With a lineup of players each with their own deep musical resumes and variety of styles to bring to the table, they've set out to create a brand new sound blending reggae, rock, hip-hop, & funk together into an electric sound. 

They Released their Debut EP, "Music Is The Medicine" in 2023 and have performed shows in Utah, Nevada, California, & Florida in the last 4 years. Their newest single “I’ll Be Ok” was released 2/27/26.

Always with the crowd in mind, they bring all of their energy to their performances with one singular goal - to get you on your feet, out of your mind, and into the music. events.title: Opera Around the World: Peru | Free Concert — Park City Opera
events.description:
Centuries of passion are distilled into one summer evening. From the golden age of bel canto to the drama of Puccini, this is opera at its most classic and irresistible.

A rare, stunning program you won't find anywhere else, featuring two Peruvian singers who bring classical vocal settings of Latin American folksongs to life. This program reminds us how wide the world of opera truly is!

Set against Park City's stunning mountain backdrop, we invite you to bring a picnic, gather your loved ones \(dogs included\), and enjoy opera and vocal chamber music in an open-air, relaxed community setting. This event is free and no tickets are required....

events.title: American Opera at Promontory's Shed Amphitheater — Park City Opera
events.description:
Join Park City Opera for a special outdoor performance at Promontory Club's terraced amphitheater, The Shed. This concert features our full chamber orchestra, offering a rare opportunity to hear the orchestra outside of our mainstage productions, alongside a program of American opera.

Settle in with a picnic, bring your own beverages, and enjoy the evening from the lawn with friends and family. The setting is as memorable as the music, with sweeping views and open-air acoustics that make for a distinctly Park City experience.

Tickets for Promontory Club members will be available via the member portal beginning in May. Tickets for non-members are available on a first-come, first-served basis through Park City Opera now, with optional picnic basket add-ons....

events.title: American Music in Provo: Presented by The A-Frame Concert Series — Park City Opera
events.description:
Tucked into Provo's Tree Streets neighborhood, The A-Frame is one of Utah's most unusual musical spaces: a midcentury-modern home built around an acoustically rich performance space. Part family home, part concert venue, it becomes the backdrop for an intimate evening of American art song and opera with Park City Opera.

Featuring nationally acclaimed singers from across the country, this performance offers a rare opportunity to experience operatic voices up close in a warm, 40-seat setting. Featuring selections from Aaron Copland's The Tender Land and other highlights of the American vocal tradition, join us for an evening of powerful, personal storytelling that captures the heart of the American spirit....

Back for its third season, Opera on the Patio is a free outdoor performance at the Park City Library. Bring a picnic, your family, and even your dog for an evening of beloved opera set against the backdrop of a summer sunset. Enjoy great music in a relaxed, welcoming setting \(no tickets required\).

events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Haydn, Mendelssohn, & A Viola Concerto
events.description: The rich voice of the viola takes center stage as Utah Symphony Principal Viola Brant Bayless performs Dai Fujikura’s Wayfinder, a concerto inspired by Polynesian navigation traditions. In this imaginative work, the orchestra becomes the natural forces used to find direction—stars, winds, and ocean swells—while the soloist listens and charts a path forward through shifting musical currents. Like a musical guide through the evening, Maurice Cohn conducts a program that unfolds across vivid sonic landscapes, beginning with Mendelssohn’s atmospheric The Hebrides, which evokes Scotland’s rugged coastline with dark, rolling lines in the low strings set against shimmering winds and strings that suggest the sea air. The journey concludes with Haydn’s jubilant Symphony No. 104—his final symphony—whose finale bursts with rustic, folk-inspired energy that resonates beautifully in the intimate acoustics of St. Mary’s Catholic Church....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: If there’s one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it’s Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the “Ode to Joy,” as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner—an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven’s Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season....
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Mozart’s “Jupiter”
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart’s second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. Framing the concerto are Mozart’s two final symphonies—written just weeks apart—revealing the fascinating journey of the composer's musical maturation and his unmistakable brilliance. One of Mozart’s most recognizable works, his Symphony No. 40 uses a minor key—a rare choice for the composer—to build emotional intensity and dark lyricism. In contrast, the program concludes with the joyful Symphony No. 41, whose grandeur and musical tour de force ultimately earned it the nickname “Jupiter,” honoring the Roman god....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” where sweet memories offer fleeting comfort after a lover’s betrayal. Famously called “the apotheosis of the dance itself” by Wagner, Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7 brings the night to a triumphant close. An instant symphonic favorite—with early audiences reportedly demanding a repeat of the second movement—the piece surges with wildness and joy, culminating in one of the most electrifying finales in classical music....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: RVR 1/2 Marathon & 7K Trail Run
events.description: The Round Valley Rambler offers two distances, 7K or a trail half marathon. The half marathon distance is the first race in the Triple Trail Challenge.    100% of the course takes place on buff single and double track trails within the 2,100 acre, Round Valley Open Space, with altitude ranging from 6,500 to 7,100 feet. While athletes will find the trails foot-friendly, the rolling, high-desert terrain presents a few long and steady climbs followed by fast downhills.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Moose on the Loose Kid #1
events.description: Join us for Moose on the Loose 2026, a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are June 13, July 18, August 15, and September 12, with all races beginning at 9:00 AM. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20. We believe kids can do hard things while having fun. Our races are designed to be achievable and enjoyable for all levels of runners, with the goal of inspiring kids to do their best, push themselves, and believe in themselves....
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Park City Trail Series 10K
events.description: Fill your summer calendar with trail races! Park City Trail Series is a 3-race series that runs from June – August. It starts with a 5K and works up to a half marathon. The courses are forgiving enough for new trail runners but bring the excitement that seasoned trail runners love. You can mix and match which races you would like to sign up for or get all three at an amazing price! Plus, you can 10% per person by signing up 4 or more people for the series. So, get your family, friends, neighbors, or whoever you need to recruit, and get signed up today! THERE ARE NO DEFERRALS OR REFUNDS.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Women's Epic 10K at Deer Valley
events.description: Women's Epic Race at Deer Valley Resort is a 10k loop with a 1335ft vertical trek to the peak and back down. You can hike, jog, or run your journey through gorgeous trees with changing fall leaves and steep climbs. You will undoubtedly come off the mountain with more confidence and grit after completing this race to the top of the world-class Deer Valley Resort located in Park City Utah. And when you get to the finish line you can grab a drink and celebrate!
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Rock-n-Roll Train
events.description: The Rock-n-Roll Train is a 90 minute round trip ride on the historic Heber Train featuring great rock-n-roll music, trivia, and train history hosted by Mark Nelson, Executive Director. A great date night! You'll be singing along to some of the greatest rock hits ever. This year we will have a 21-and-older car with beer and wine available. You are not allowed to bring alcohol on the train! And, when the train returns you can stick around for a live concert featuring The Flashback Brothers. There will be food and drink and lots of good times at the Depot after the train! The onboard Train Bar will be open during the concert. Alcohol must be consumed on the train.

Swiss Days includes a 10K race, parade, live entertainment, and so much more. DATES: Friday, September 4 & Saturday, September 5, 2026 SCHEDULE: Friday, Sep 4th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Saturday, Sep 5th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Breakfast & 10K Run start at 7:00 am, Parade at 10:00 am PARKING: Two public parking locations offer a free shuttle bus to and from the event location. Parking is $10 and the shuttle runs approximately every 15 minutes. There is limited street parking and private pay parking lots surrounding the square.‍ Complimentary bike parking is available at Centennial Park. PETS: Pets are NOT allowed at the venue.‍

events.title: Dominick Antonelli @ Cafe Galleria
events.description: Live Summer Music Series May through September (weather permitting) Every day but Mondays from 5:30pm – 8:30pm Sundays 10am – 1:30pm Dominick has been playing guitar for 21 years and writing original music for 15+ of those years. He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health and is currently studying Music and Recording Arts Technology which is taking his creative possibilities to new depths and heights. He has performed at festivals in Thailand, Costa Rica, and the East Coast (US), as well as in yoga classes, local gigs, and workshops where he incorporates live music, meditation, group energy healing and writing into one experience. He loves performing live and sharing the gifts that continue to be cultivated. Read more about Dominick
events.location: Cafe Galleria, 101 West Main Street

events.title: Saturday Sunset Music Series
events.description: There’s no better way to spend a summer evening in Heber City than at the Saturday Sunset Music Series. Every Saturday from 7:00–8:30 PM, Main Street Park comes alive with live music, good food, and a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere as the sun sets over downtown. Saturday programming is part of the broader Music on Main season. The series features a diverse lineup of local musicians, highlighting the depth of talent in the Heber Valley.
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wine Tasting %26 Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leisure. You'll ride in one of our beautiful vintage lounge cars. Tickets are sold either by the half table for 2 people (may be shared with 2 other guests), a full table for 4, and a limited number of single seats. Wines on the menu: Decoy Brut Cuvee Kumusha Sauvignon Blanc Cest La Vie Rouge (Pinot Noir & Syrah Blend) Larchago Tempranillo - Rioja, Spain Graham's Six Grapes Ruby Port
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Midway America 250 Celebrations
events.description: June 15TH - July 11TH: Memorial Hill Flag Legacy Event As part of Midway City’s America250 celebration, the Flag Legacy Event tribute project will place 250 flags along the trail on Memorial Hill in honor of Wasatch County veterans. Together, these flags will create a meaningful and visible memorial. The display will honor those who have served our country, recognize the freedoms made possible through their sacrifice, and celebrate the 250th anniversary of America. The Memorial Hill Flag posting ceremony will take place on June 15th start at 6 pm at the Midway Town Hall at 75 N 100 W.
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Midway America 250 Celebrations 4th of July
events.description: There’s nothing quite like music in the mountains on the 4th of July. 🇺🇸🎶 As part of Midway’s America250 celebration we welcome you to join with us for an unforgettable evening with the Utah Symphony. It will be an evening filled with music that celebrates America. 📅 Saturday, July 4, 2026 🕗 8:00 PM 📍 Midway Town Square 🎟 Free to attend Bring your blankets, chairs and your patriotic spirit-this is a night you won’t want to miss!
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Road to the Million Championship Night
events.description: The Old West Futurity is set to return to Heber Valley June 20, bringing some of the nation’s top horse trainers, competitors, owners, and equine athletes to the Wasatch County Event Complex for elite competition and western tradition. The competition culminates with the Road to the Million Championship Night on Saturday, June 20, featuring elite Heading, Heeling, and Breakaway competitors competing for more than $2.5 million in prizes, including the chance to win a $1 million bonus. The evening will also include live performances by western entertainers Dave Stamey and Gunnar Latham. Date: Saturday, June 20, 2026 Location: Wasatch County Event Complex, Heber City Gates Open: 6:00 PM Opening Ceremonies: 7:00 PM Tickets: $35 General Admission | Children 5 & Under Free For tickets and additional event information, visit: https://www.oldwest.com/pages/heber-finals-and-concert

Bring your friends and get connected at this free, community-wide celebration and fair. Be sure to stop by our tables to learn about Park City Community Foundation and our initiatives including Early Childhood Alliance, Youth United, Zero Food Waste, Women’s Giving Fund, and Community Pass Club – and how you can contribute and get involved. events.title: Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health
events.description: Onyx Club is proud to present a focused conversation on what truly drives long-term health, performance, and longevity. Join Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, Co-founder of The Smart Fit Method for a focused discussion on the key drivers of longevity and health optimization. In this session they will break down the Four Pillars of Longevity – sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress – and how they translate into real, measurable outcomes in your daily life. This talk is about understanding what actually works and how to apply it in a realistic, sustainable way. You’ll learn: How to focus on the key drivers that have the biggest impact on your health and longevity How advanced testing and diagnostics (lab work, body composition, VO₂ max, and more) can personalize your approach What data actually matters and how tracking the right metrics can improve performance and long-term health How to implement these principles within a busy, real-life schedule Connor will also share how these concepts come to life through the Smart Fit Method—a highly efficient, science-based training approach built from his own journey of injury, recovery, and rebuilding strength. After experiencing severe physical setbacks early in life, beginning his journey as a professional skier, he developed a system focused on helping others recover, perform, and extend their health span. Dr. Duerler brings over two decades of experience in family medicine, now focused on proactive, prevention-based care. His work centers on helping individuals preserve vitality, function, and cognitive performance through personalized, data-informed strategies. Together, they offer a grounded, practical perspective on how to take control of your health without overcomplicating it. The evening will include a brief presentation, followed by Q&A and time to connect with light bites and refreshments. Perks Attendees receive 15% off at the Overlook Restaurant at Black Rock Mountain Resort ...

events.title: Solstice Volunteer + Beer (21+)
events.description: Celebrate the solstice and longest day of the year with Swaner! Help us tackle invasive weeds on the Preserve and improve habitat quality and the wetland ecosystem. Meet at the Swaner EcoCenter: extension.usu.edu/swaner/directions Volunteers will head out onto the Preserve to pull invasive weeds, and afterwards head back to the EcoCenter to enjoy the back deck from 7 – 8pm. Pizza, beer, and non-alcoholic seltzers will be provided to all volunteers. YOU MUST REGISTER TO ATTEND the event and be 21 years of age or older. ID’s will be checked at the door.
events.location: Swaner Preserve and EcoCenter, 1258 Center Drive, Park City

events.title: Plein Air Painting: The Living Landscape
events.description: Join painter Johnny DeFeo for three immersive days of plein air exploration, where landscape becomes both subject and teacher. Rather than a traditional “paint-along,” instruction is intentionally intimate and responsive, with frequent mini-critiques and one-on-one guidance. With short lectures and demonstrations all on location, develop your own visual language and body of work. Participants should bring their own supplies and have prior painting experience. This workshop is designed for artists ready to deepen their relationship with landscape and push their work beyond observation into expression....

events.description: Thrive is a FREE evidence-based program equipping Summit & Wasatch County students in grades 6-12 with vital life skills. events.title: Demonstration Garden Volunteer
events.description: Help bees, butterflies, and other pollinators by assisting with our demonstration garden in front of the EcoCenter! events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community gatherings, and a Native Fashion Show. events.title: Rendezvous Mountain Hill Climb
events.description: The most demanding hill climb in the West is back! Set for August 1, the race kicks off at the base of Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, climbs up the Wildflower Trail to Rendezvous Lodge, transitions onto the Cirque Trail, and finishes at the summit of the Aerial Tram at the top of Rendezvous Bowl. This annual nonprofit race is held in honor of our friend and longtime JHMR Aerial Tram mechanic, Chris Onufer (Captain “X”), who tragically passed away in an avalanche on March 7, 2012. Everyone is welcome, whether you’re a seasoned trail runner or just out for a challenging hike! Registration coming soon. 2026 Race Details

events.title: Jackson Hole Half Marathon
events.description: The Jackson Hole Half Marathon follows an amazing 13.1 mile course along the foot of the Tetons from Teton Village to Downto wn Jackson. You’ll follow a path that is a fast, net downhill route that has been described as one of the best altitude courses in the nation. So take up the challenge and prepare yourself now. Because if the elevation doesn’t take your breath away, the incredible scenery will! 13.1 Mile Race: The Jackson Hole Half Marathon The race will begin just south of Teton Village on the bike path. Runners will follow the bike path, towards the town of Jackson, for approximately 11 ½ miles. The course will then go toward the finish at Phil Baux Park, 10 E. Snow King Ave. JH Half will provide buses to transport runners and walkers from Phil Baux Park (finish line) to the start at 6:00 am. DATE: Saturday, June 13, 2026 TIME: 7:00 am – course closes at 11:00 am START: SOUTH OF TETON VILLAGE FINISH: PHIL BAUX PARK, 10 E. SNOW KING AVE 5K Rad Run The 5K Rad Run is in remembrance of our beloved community member, Rad Spencer, that we lost in a skiing accident February 13, 2022. Proceeds from this race will be contributed to 2 entities that Rad was deeply connected to: Jackson Hole Youth Lacrosse and Fresh Life Church. DATE: Saturday, June 13, 2026 RUN START TIME: 7:00am START & FINISH: PHIL BAUX PARK, 10 E. SNOW KING AVE...

events.title: Jackson Hole Food & Wine Summer Festival
events.description: Jackson Hole Food & Wine offers a variety of flavors and experiences unique to Jackson Hole. Join us this June to interact and dine with a broad culinary community of local and guest chefs, winemakers, cocktail and beer experts and much more. Taste why Jackson Hole has become an impressive food and wine destination. 2026 Schedule Of Events Thursday, June 25th TASTE OF JACKSON HOLE Taste of Jackson Hole offers guests an exclusive chance to taste a diverse range of signature dishes prepared by the best of Jackson Hole’s culinary community. Arrive via Bridger Gondola, take in Jackson Hole Mountain Resort’s stunning views from the top of the mountain, and taste mouthwatering bites from the finest local restaurants, paired with a curated selection of beer, wine and spirits. Join us for one of Jackson’s most anticipated summer events! Tickets: https://www.jhfoodandwine.com/summer-festival Location: Rendezvous Lodge via Bridger Gondola Time: 6:00 – 9:00pm Friday, June 26 NOBU NEW YORK + VINEYARDS BRANDS Join a rare and intimate culinary experience at the iconic WRJ Headquarters at Stags Landing, Jackson Hole. Hosted by WRJ founders Rush Jenkins and Klaus Baer in partnership with Zions Bank, this evening welcomes just 40 guests to an extraordinary wine dinner. Savor the world-renowned flavors of Nobu New York, led by Chef Matt Hoyle, in a multi-course menu blending Japanese-Peruvian fusion with pristine seafood, bold yet elegant flavors, and meticulous artistry. Each course will be expertly paired by Jason Sorrell of Vineyard Brands, highlighting exceptional wines that elevate and harmonize with the cuisine. Adding to the evening’s distinction, Nobu founder Drew Nieporent will also be present, offering a rare opportunity to connect with the vision behind one of the world’s most influential restaurant groups. Tickets: https://www.jhfoodandwine.com/summer-festival Location: WRJ Headquarters | Stags Landing Time: 6:30 – 9:30pm CHEF GAVIN KAYSEN + HIRSCH VINEYARDS For one ni...
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, 3395 Cody Ln, Teton Village, WY

events.title: Grand Teton Music Festival
events.description: Join us for Grand Teton Music Festival’s 2026 Season: July 2nd – August 15th The 65th season includes eight weeks of performances under the leadership of Music Director Sir Donald Runnicles Season 65 features seven weeks of world-class music led by Music Director Sir Donald Runnicles. This anniversary season celebrates the stunning natural beauty of Jackson Hole as our home, Walk Festival Hall, undergoes a transformative renovation. Classical gems and renowned soloists will grace the Festival stages as the Grand Teton Music Festival “camps out” at venues including the Jackson Hole High School Auditorium, the Center for the Arts, Teton Village Commons, and more. Season 65 features performances by Capathia Jenkins, Daniel Luis Espinal, Eleni Calenos, Conrad Jones, The King’s Singers, Madeline Adkins, Maria Ioudenitch, the Punch Brothers and more. Masterworks of symphonic and chamber music grace GTMF’s stages including Beethoven’s Symphony No. 6, Rachmaninoff’s Piano Concerto No. 2, excerpts from Vivaldi’s The Four Seasons and much more....

events.title: Teton County Fair
events.description: Celebrate the 70th Annual Teton County Fair from July 18 – 26, 2026. The iconic festival includes a carnival, concerts, the rodeo, livestock shows and auctions, contests, 50+ vendors, and more. The Teton County Fair is one of Jackson Hole’s most anticipated annual events! The Teton County Fair spans nine days, features entertaining events, shows, and programming, and draws competitors and spectators from all demographics. It’s our goal to produce an exceptional and memorable experience for all who join us. We’re bringing back the favorites, including the Horse Show competitions, Frazier Shows carnival, 4-H livestock shows and sale, All Aboard Train, Freckle Farm Petting Zoo, the Blue Ribbon Exhibit Hall, Fair Rodeo, and Figure 8 Races. We’re excited to announce a new Wednesday night event this year, Extreme Medieval Jousting! The Fair Concert will move to Thursday night in the Rodeo Arena. We’re also adding laser tag on the tennis courts, which is free and fun for all ages. In addition to the Lion’s Club Breakfast and Beer Garden, the Big Top Tent will feature new stage programming and strolling entertainment. Enjoy a variety of live music, participate in a jackpot cornhole tournament, partake in the nationally-recognized pie eating contest, enter your newborn in the diaper derby, and bring your wiener dog to the races. The Blue Ribbon Exhibit Hall will now be located in the new Fairgrounds Community Building behind the Fair Office. All department “Best of Show” winners will be awarded a trophy belt buckle this year....
events.location: Teton County Fair, 305 West Snow King Ave, Jackson, WY

events.title: Plein Air Fest, Etc.
events.description: Nearly 50 invited artists are given one day to create new masterpieces en plein air (outside). They paint from the National Museum of Wildlife Art’s Sculpture Trail with stupendous views overlooking the National Elk Refuge. All visitors are welcome to watch the painters, and collectors are invited to browse and bid on the fresh artworks. All proceeds will benefit the Museum and participating artists. Enjoy free admission from 9 a.m. – 1 p.m. 9 – 10:30 a.m.: New this year – Coffee, mimosas, and pastries for purchase from Palate 10 a.m. – 1 p.m.: Live Music from Derrik & The Dynamos 11 a.m. – 1 p.m.: Food for Purchase from Palate 12:15 – 1 p.m.: Silent Auction 2026 Participating Artists: Taryn Boals Emily Boespflug Pat Branting Allen B. Brockbank Nikita Budkov Jan Bushart Jude Chilen Pamm Ciupa Charles Dayton Dave English Katy Ann Fox Nicolette Gdontakis Maw Marcella Gillenwater Roberta Glidden Patricia A. Griffin Alissa Hartmann Jennifer L. Hoffman DG House Aaron Cordell Johnson Gary J. Keimig Carole Keller Fred Kingwill Laurie LaMere Haley Leeper Les LeFevre Gabriel Lopez Blake Luther Elise Mahaffie Robert Martinez Roberta Miller Doug Monson Matt Montagne Cindy Moore Cathy Sue Munson Erin C. O’Connor Mae Orm Nolan Parson Amy Ringholz Shannon Marie Schacht Rachel Smith Kay Stratman Andrew Taylor Silas Thompson Sue Tyler Benjamin Walter Kathy KJ Wipfler Marina Zavalova Two participation categories for the artists: plein air and al fresco The plein air category allows artists to paint any regional scene of their choice, and they may begin their painting anywhere in the vicinity. All artists will be on the Sculpture Trail by 10 a.m. to finish their piece live and in person. Al fresco is interpreted more broadly, where artists enjoy painting outside using reference photos of any subject. The “Best in Show” award winner will be picked from the plein air category, while the al fresco category is not judged. This event is generously sponsored by Barbara Carlsberg, Petria...
events.location: National Museum of Wildlife Art, 2820 Rungius Road, Jackson, WY, 83001

events.title: Walk-Up Wednesdays
events.description: Join us for a free guided tour at 11 a.m. every Wednesday through August! This engaging tour is led by a trained Museum guide and offers extraordinary opportunities for visitors to make the most of their encounters with great works of art. Walk-Up Wednesday tours are included in your membership or the cost of your admission ticket for that day. If you would like to join us on a tour, please meet in Sullivan Hall by our Walk-Up Wednesdays sign just before 11 a.m. The tour will be 45 minutes long and is filled on a first-come, first served basis.
events.location: Jackson, WY

events.title: Fables, Feathers & Fur
events.description: Fun for ages 3-6 Storytelling and art-making at the museum is an exciting opportunity for young visitors to engage with the art through looking, reading, and art-making in the galleries. All materials are provided. Walk-ins welcome! Registration is only required for groups of 10 or more. We can also accommodate educators interested in scheduling Fables, Feathers & Fur programming for their respective groups. For more information and to schedule your group, please contact kjhahn@wildlifeart.org . Generously sponsored by Nancy & Dick Collister.
events.location: Jackson, WY

events.title: Yoga on the Trail
events.description: Thursdays in July & August Top Jackson Hole yoga instructors conduct weekly summer classes outside on the south part of the Sculpture Trail (parking lot to your left as you come up the hill). Participants will feel inspired doing Sun Salutations and Downward Dogs as they look across the grand vista of the National Elk Refuge. Join us in connecting with two art forms—visual art inspired by artists over the centuries, and the art of yoga, ensuring healthy mind, body, and spirit. All levels welcome. Free. *Please bring your own mat and water!

events.title: Make it Wild: Bison Glass Painting
events.description: Join artist Hayley Ruland for a fun and creative Paint By Glass workshop! In this hands-on class, participants will create their own wildlife-inspired landscape featuring a bison using mosaic glass and wood. You’ll bring your piece to life by selecting from a variety of glass colors to design your unique landscape. You’ll learn how to nip and glue glass into the custom 3D wooden palette to create a unique mixed-media artwork full of color and texture. No prior glass experience is needed, just come ready to create and leave with a one-of-a-kind piece inspired by the beauty of Wyoming. This class is presented alongside the exhibition Bison-tennial: From Colonization to Rematriation . A museum educator will lead a close looking tour for the first 30 minutes of the class to learn more about and reflect on the bison. The next 3 hours will conclude with art making in the classroom. This class will take place from 10 a.m. – 1:30 p.m. on July 25, in the Museum Classroom. Register Now! Make it Wild courses are open to adults and young adults with beginner to intermediate abilities. Participants will have the opportunity to ask questions during this interactive program. $60 per Live In-Person Class Limited to the first 20 participants Your registration fee supports the Museum’s Art Leadership Scholarship in Honor of the Memory of Dick Jennings. Dick Jennings was a Museum board member whose memory inspires us to pursue our passions. The $4,000 scholarship is awarded annually to a local High School Senior who plans to study art at the college level. Thank you for your support! About the artist: Hayley Ruland lives in Pinedale and began working with glass art in high school and hasn’t been able to stop since. With a background in civil engineering, she enjoys combining creativity with technical problem-solving by incorporating glass fusing, mixed media, and custom laser-cut wood elements that add depth and dimension to her work. She loves sharing her passion for glass with ot...

events.title: Walk-Up Wednesdays
events.description: Join us for a free guided tour at 11 a.m. every Wednesday through August! This engaging tour is led by a trained Museum guide and offers extraordinary opportunities for visitors to make the most of their encounters with great works of art. Walk-Up Wednesday tours are included in your membership or the cost of your admission ticket for that day. If you would like to join us on a tour, please meet in Sullivan Hall by our Walk-Up Wednesdays sign just before 11 a.m. The tour will be 45 minutes long and is filled on a first-come, first-served basis.
events.location: Jackson, WY

events.title: Yoga on the Trail
events.description: Thursdays in July & August Top Jackson Hole yoga instructors conduct weekly summer classes outside on the south part of the Sculpture Trail (parking lot to your left as you come up the hill). Participants will feel inspired doing Sun Salutations and Downward Dogs as they look across the grand vista of the National Elk Refuge. Join us in connecting with two art forms—visual art inspired by artists over the centuries, and the art of yoga, ensuring healthy mind, body, and spirit. All levels welcome. Free. *Please bring your own mat and water!

events.title: Grizzly 399 Sculpture Unveiling
events.description: Join us for the unveiling and dedication of the Grizzly 399 Monument , to be held on September 9, 2026, at 5 p.m. The Grizzly 399 Monument was created under the artistic leadership of Jocelyn Russell; a distinguished wildlife sculptor whose body of work includes more than sixty public monuments recognized for their expressive realism. Kristen Douglas-Seitz and Colleen Moore contributed to the project as members of the sculpting team. The monument, showing 399 and her famous quads, will be permanently installed near the museum’s front entrance, where it will be accessible to the public. Its placement provides an enduring setting to honor Grizzly 399, one of the most widely recognized wildlife figures of the Greater Yellowstone Ecosystem, within a cultural institution devoted to wildlife art and conservation storytelling. The public dedication event is free and open to all and will include remarks, an artist meet-and-greet, and educational programming: 5 – 5:30 p.m. — Dedication ceremony and remarks 5:30 – 6 p.m. — Artist meet-and-greet 6 – 7 p.m. — Public presentation in the museum auditorium, featuring insight into the creation of the sculpture and a question-and-answer session with the artist Complimentary food and beverages will be available between 5 and 6 p.m. Enlarged rendering/mock-up of the clay maquette....
events.location: National Museum of Wildlife Art, 2820 Rungius Road, Jackson, WY

events.title: 39th Western Visions® Evening Sale
events.description: Western Visions ® is the National Museum of Wildlife Art’s largest and longest-running fundraiser and is one of the signature events of the Jackson Hole Fall Arts Festival. Top contemporary wildlife artists and collectors from around the world will come together at the Museum in Jackson Hole, Wyoming. The show features a wide selection of art for sale and supports participating artists and all we do here at the Museum. The week’s events, offered at the Museum’s award-winning facility across from the National Elk Refuge, draw an international crowd to this critically acclaimed gathering. During Fall Arts Festival (and year-round), the Museum is a hub for art lovers, wildlife and outdoor enthusiasts, and more. Tickets go on sale August 1, 2026. events.title: Mata Ortiz Pop-Up Pottery Shop
events.description: Discover the beauty of Mata Ortiz pottery! Second generation ceramic artist Oralia Lopez is renowned for her finely executed geometric pottery designs, often inspired by nature. Lopez will be hosting a pottery pop-up in Sullivan Hall, outside of the Museum Shop July 17 – 19. Originally from the village of Mata Ortiz in northern Chihuahua, Mexico, Lopez is part of a group of artisans carrying forward a centuries-old ceramic tradition rooted in the prehistoric Mogollon culture and the archaeological site of Casas Grandes. Join us for artist demonstrations throughout the pottery pop-up event included in your admission ticket or Museum membership. Schedule: Friday, July 17 – 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. Saturday, July 18 – 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. Sunday, July 19 – 10 a.m. to 4 p.m....

events.title: Bike Park Opening Day
events.description: Pump up those tires and dust off your bike, it’s opening day at the Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park! With terrain for every rider, from novice to pro, the park offers everything from beginner-friendly flow trails to expert tabletops and high-speed features. Catch your breath while you can; the Sweetwater Gondola and Teewinot Chair will take you right back up for another lap. Don’t miss the start of an epic summer of riding!
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, Teton Village, Wyoming , 83025

events.title: John Sidle Live on The Deck
events.description: Experience live music at 9,095 feet on The Deck . Take a scenic ride up the Bridger Gondola or hike your way up, then settle in for an evening of great tunes, unbeatable views, and refreshing food and drinks. This free, all-summer series features a rotating lineup of artists in a relaxed, open-air alpine setting.
events.location: Top of the Bridger Gondola, Teton Village, WY, 83025

events.title: Sunday Funday
events.description: Say goodbye to the Sunday scaries with Sunday Funday at Jackson Hole Mountain Resort. From 2:00 – 5:00 PM, enjoy $20 bike park lift tickets, 50% off bike rentals, and 20% off bike park guides. Enjoy special dining discounts at Tram Dock and Tin Can Cantina, then stick around for live music at Concerts on the Commons. You’re also welcome to bring your own picnic. Outside food and beverages are allowed, please leave the glass and dogs at home. See more information below. More Info
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, Teton Village, Wyoming, 83025

events.title: Ladies' Laps - Cornering
events.description: Ladies' Laps creates a space for ladies aged 18+ to enhance their downhill bike skills and meet other lady bikers in the community. Basic bike skills are recommended as these clinics are designed to enhance skills and build on basics. Ladies Laps makes the most of the 2-hour bike park session with lift-access small group instruction (max 5 people) where personal coaching is a focus. Meet female bikers who share a passion for outdoor thrills and walk away with the skills needed to tackle more technical riding. Learn More
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park, Teton Village , WY, 83025
events.venue_name: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park

events.title: ILLA Women's Mountain Bike Day
events.description: ILLA and Jackson Hole Mountain Resort are teaming up for a women's takeover of the Jackson Hole Bike Park! There will be downhill b ike demo discounts, a happy hour at Tram Dock, raffles, plus all ILLA members will ride the Park for FREE courtesy of JHMR. Never been to a bike park before? This event is for you! Get free lift tickets + discounted rentals ! Want to meet more women to ride with? We've got some friends we'd like you to meet. Join us for a group ride! Always wanted to check out the Jackson Hole Bike Park? We want to high-five you in the lift line. Load up the car and bring all your friends! There are trails for all abilities. Don't ride park? Come anyway! There are a few very accessible green trails, or enjoy the hiking trails then apres! Sign Up
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park, Jackson, WY, 83025

events.title: JH Sports Whip Off and Chainless Race
events.description: Get ready to send it at the JH Sports Whip Off and Chainless Race! Registration will take place in person at JH Sports from 12:30 - 1:30 PM. The chainless race will take place at 2:00 on Bronco in a test of creativity, momentum, and flow. The whip off will go down immediately after on Sweet Timothy Jumps, where style points reign supreme. Use the terrain to your advantage, and battle for a chance to claim a top spot. Divisions include Youth (15 and under) and Adult (16 and over).
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park, Teton Village, WY , 83025
events.venue_name: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park

events.title: Jackson Hole Food & Wine | Summer Festival
events.description: Join us for the Summer Festival—a three-day culinary celebration set against the breathtaking backdrop of Jackson Hole. This year we are excited to celebrate our 10-year anniversary! Join us as we toast to a decade of memorable flavors in Jackson Hole. Enjoy an unforgettable lineup of events, from the Summer Kick-Off: Taste of Jackson Hole and vibrant tastings, to Intimate Guest Chef Dinner Series and SAVOR, the ultimate party on a ranch! Meet and mingle with celebrated local and guest chefs, acclaimed winemakers, craft brewers, and beverage experts. Sip, savor, and soak in the beauty of summer while discovering why Jackson Hole has earned its place as a premier food and beverage destination. Buy Tickets
events.location: Jackson Hole, WY, Jackson Hole, WY, 83001

events.title: Jackson Hole Food & Wine | Anything But Chardonnay Wine Tasting
events.description: Get Tickets Here: Summer Festival â Jackson Hole Food & Wine Join us for a blind tasting experience that challenges everything you thought you knew about the world's most misunderstood white grape. We will explore how region, climate, and winemaking philosophy can transform a single variety into wildly different expressions. Through a curated flight of eight wines, you will discover what terroir, technique, and intention actually taste like in the glass. Whether you are a devoted fan or a committed skeptic, this tasting is designed to sharpen your palate and just might change your mind. Come with an open mind. Leave with a new favorite pour! Please Note: Must be 21 or older to attend any Jackson Hole Food & Wine event No ticket refunds or exchanges Due to our location in the mountains we occasionally experience delays due to weather or other factors. If an event is delayed, but not cancelled due to unforeseen circumstances, we will not offer refunds.
events.location: Four Seasons Resort & Residences JH, Teton Village, WY, 83025

events.title: Jackson Hole Food & Wine | Taste of Jackson Hole | Presented by MLD
events.description: Get Tickets Here: Summer Festival â Jackson Hole Food & Wine Experience the Heart of Jackson Holeâs Culinary Community! Taste of Jackson Hole offers a unique opportunity to connect with the culinary community that makes this valley so special. Guests will sample signature dishes from many of Jackson Holeâs top chefs and restaurants, each showcasing the creativity and passion that define the local dining scene. Arrive via the iconic Bridger Gondola and take in sweeping mountain views from the top of Jackson Hole Mountain Resort while savoring exceptional bites from beloved local restaurants alongside outstanding beverages from both local producers and distinguished wine and spirits makers from around the world. More than just a tasting, this lively evening celebrates the people, flavors, and stories behind Jackson Holeâs thriving food culture. Join us for one of the valleyâs most anticipated summer culinary events. Please Note: Must be 21 or older to attend any Jackson Hole Food & Wine event No ticket refunds or exchanges In order for us to allow our participating chefs full creativity, our events are not catered to those with strict dietary restrictions or allergies. Our events are held rain or shine (or snow, which happens sometimes even during our summer events!). If an event is delayed, but not cancelled due to unforeseen circumstances, we will not offer refunds.
events.location: Rendezvous Lodge via Bridger Gondola, Teton Village, WY, 83025

events.title: Jackson Hole Food & Wine | Dinner Series | Chef Jonathan Waxman + Walter Scott Wines
events.description: Get Tickets Here: Summer Festival â Jackson Hole Food & Wine Join us at the breathtaking Snake River Sporting Club for an unforgettable evening with legendary chef Jonathan Waxman of Barbuto . A pioneer of California cuisine and one of Americaâs most influential chefs, Waxman is celebrated for his vibrant, ingredient-driven cooking that blends French technique with the rustic flavors of the Italian countryside. For this special dinner, he will present a seasonal multi-course menu showcasing his signature style of simple, beautiful dishes built around exceptional ingredients. Each course will be thoughtfully paired with wines from Walter Scott Wines , the acclaimed Willamette Valley producer known for its elegant, terroir-driven Chardonnays and Pinot Noirs. Winemaker Ken Pahlow and founder Erica Landon will be in attendance to share insights about the wines and the pairings as each course is served. Limited to just 64 guests, this intimate evening promises extraordinary food and remarkable wines set against the stunning backdrop of Snake River Sporting Club. Please Note: Must be 21 or older to attend any Jackson Hole Food & Wine event No ticket refunds or exchanges In order for us to allow our participating chefs full creativity, our dinners do not cater to those with strict dietary restrictions or allergies. If you have any questions, please email haynes@jhfoodandwine.com If an event is delayed, but not cancelled due to unforeseen circumstances, we will not offer refunds.
events.location: WRJ Headquarters | Stags Landing, Jackson, WY, 83001

events.title: Ladies' Laps - Roots & Rocks
events.description: Ladies' Laps creates a space for ladies aged 18+ to enhance their downhill bike skills and meet other lady bikers in the community. Basic bike skills are recommended as these clinics are designed to enhance skills and build on basics. Ladies Laps makes the most of the 2-hour bike park session with lift-access small group instruction (max 5 people) where personal coaching is a focus. Meet female bikers who share a passion for outdoor thrills and walk away with the skills needed to tackle more technical riding. Learn More
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park, Teton Village , WY, 83025
events.venue_name: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park

events.title: JH Downhill Championships Race #1 (Solitoga Short Track)
events.description: This three-part downhill series is the perfect way to get to know, or reconnect with some of JHMR’s best bike park lines. Open to riders ages 12 through 40+, it’s a great chance to test your technical skills alongside a solid field of competitors. Looking to step it up? The pro divisions bring serious competition, with a $1,000 prize for the overall series winner. More details to come.
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, Teton Village, Wyoming , 83025

events.description: Join Rendezvous Run Club at Teton Village Sports for a Saturday morning community run! Test out the latest shoes from Altra with their demo fleet available to try. Post-run beverages will be provided at TVS, so stick around, meet new friends, and kick off your Saturday strong!

events.title: Jackson Hole Food & Wine | Savor
events.description: Get Tickets Here: Summer Festival â Jackson Hole Food & Wine Step into your finest western attire and join us for an extraordinary afternoon of flavor and festivity. The festivalâs acclaimed guest chefs Gavin Kaysen , Matt Hoyle , and Jonathan Waxman will join forces with Jackson Holeâs premier culinary talent for the ultimate western walk-around tasting. Delight in an exquisite collection of small plates, each artfully crafted to highlight the chefâs passion and signature style. Raise your glass with a selection of crisp wines, refreshing beers, and craft cocktails as you mingle with fellow food and wine enthusiasts. Jackson Holeâs favorite Bluegrass band, One Ton Pig , will fill the air setting the perfect backdrop for this unforgettable gathering. Adding a touch of classic Western style, Sing Hat Company will offer handcrafted custom hats, Premium kitchenware from the Staub and Zwilling Airstream, and a taste of Wyoming-rooted adventure from Airstream of Wyoming. Donât miss this chance to indulge your senses, celebrate delicious cuisine, and step fully into the true essence of the West. Please Note: Must be 21 or older to attend any Jackson Hole Food & Wine event No ticket refunds or exchanges In order for us to allow our participating chefs full creativity, our events are not catered to those with strict dietary restrictions or allergies. Our events are held rain or shine (or snow, which happens sometimes even during our summer events!). If an event is delayed, but not cancelled due to unforeseen circumstances, we will not offer refunds.

events.title: Grand Teton Music Fest 65th Season
events.description: In Summer 2026, the Grand Teton Music Festival celebrates its 65th anniversary season led by Music Director Sir Donald Runnicles in Jackson Hole, Wyoming. Featuring seven weeks of world-class music – including programs by the Festival Orchestra, three Gateway Series presentations, and five Benoliel Chamber Music Series concerts, plus free family, education, and community engagement presentations. Season 65 features performances of Tchaikovsky's Fifth Symphony, Beethoven's Sixth Symphony & Rachmaninoff's Piano Concerto No. 2 and renowned soloists including Maria Ioudenitch, Madeline Adkins, Eleni Calenos, José González Granero and more. Series packages on sale now, single tickets go on sale April 7. Learn More
events.location: Jackson Hole High School Auditorium, Center for the Arts lawn + theatre, Teton Village Commons, Jackson, WY, 83001

events.title: Red, White and Tunes Friday Show
events.description: Enjoy a night of jamming with Steely Dead and special guest Brock Gleeson. Steely Dead is a Denver-based touring band that fuses the precision of Steely Dan with the improvisational spirit of the Grateful Dead. Featuring Dave Abear (guitar), Matt Abear (bass), Chris Sheldon (drums), and Dylan Teifer (keys), the group delivers tight musicianship, fluid jams, and inventive transitions. Listen Here
events.location: Teton Village, Teton Village, WY, 83025

events.title: Red, White & Tunes - Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Red, White & Tunes takes over Teton Village as the official celebration of Fourth of July in Jackson Hole, featuring three days of free live music, local vendors, family-friendly fun and classic Americana contests—all set beneath the Tetons. Each day builds toward an unforgettable Fourth of July finale: fireworks lighting up the mountain sky.
events.location: Teton Village, WY, Teton Village, Wyoming, 83025

events.title: Red, White and Tunes Saturday Show
events.description: The Honey Island Swamp Band delivers a high-energy, genre-blurring take on contemporary roots music, where melody, storytelling, and groove come first. Formed in the wake of Hurricane Katrina and rooted in New Orleans tradition, the band blends rock, blues, soul, and Americana into a sound that’s both timeless and fresh. Listen Here

events.title: MC Presents Art and Antique Show
events.description: With award winning artists, and nationally acclaimed antique dealers, MC Presents has something for everyone. Our shows are a unique blend of fine art, and one of a kind antiques, bringing you the very best quality. They are held in the beautiful, premier resort area of Teton Village, Wyoming. Located at the base of the Teton mountains, Teton Village, and Jackson Hole Mountain Resort offer a beautiful and relaxed atmosphere. A short drive from Jackson Hole, Teton Village offers activities for anyone. From a tram ride to the very top, hiking, parasailing, and even horseback rides. Come shop at the show, then enjoy lunch, dinner, or drinks at one of the many restaurants in Teton Village. We hope to see you there! Learn More

events.title: Teton Toss #11 Disc Golf Tournament
events.description: Teton Toss is back for our 11th year! Join the Teton Disc Golf Club for a single round of disc golf covering all 27 holes at Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, a massive raffle, and fun along the way. This unique tournament is half disc golf and half party so plan to make the trip out to Teton Village for a great day! Register Now More info about disc golf at JHMR
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, Teton Village, WY, 83025

events.title: Ladies' Laps - Cornering
events.description: Ladies' Laps creates a space for ladies aged 18+ to enhance their downhill bike skills and meet other lady bikers in the community. Basic bike skills are recommended as these clinics are designed to enhance skills and build on basics. Ladies Laps makes the most of the 2-hour bike park session with lift-access small group instruction (max 5 people) where personal coaching is a focus. Meet female bikers who share a passion for outdoor thrills and walk away with the skills needed to tackle more technical riding. Learn More
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park, Teton Village , WY, 83025
events.venue_name: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort Bike Park

events.description: Object Heavy is a soul-driven, funk-forward band built around deep grooves, raw musicianship, and a modern take on classic rhythm and blues. Drawing from the foundations of funk, soul, jazz, and rock, their sound is both tight and expansive—anchored in pocket-heavy rhythm sections, expressive vocals, and fearless improvisation. Listen Here

events.title: JH Downhill Championships Race #2 (Dirty Harry)
events.description: This three-part downhill series is the perfect way to get to know, or reconnect with some of JHMR’s best bike park lines. Open to riders ages 12 through 40+, it’s a great chance to test your technical skills alongside a solid field of competitors. Looking to step it up? The pro divisions bring serious competition, with a $1,000 prize for the overall series winner. Registration coming soon.
events.location: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, Teton Village, Wyoming , 83025

events.title: Fantasia
events.description: The Festival Orchestra is the heart of GTMF. While Walk Festival Hall is renovated, join us for a special night of classical favorites overlooking the Hall on Teton Village Commons, led by Music Director Sir Donald Runnicles. Tap your feet and dance along to Dance of the Hours, selections from Tchaikovsky’s Nutcracker Suite and more. Sir Donald Runnicles, conductor Deposit tickets are available for this event. Tickets with your seating assignment will be issued and sent at a later date. Buy Tickets

events.title: Opening Night: From the Stage to the Silver Screen
events.description: Celebrate the opening night of GTMF’s 65th season with the music of Broadway and film performed by the Festival Orchestra. Familiar favorites and iconic scores take center stage with themes from Carousel, Superman, Vertigo, and a celebration of iconic American film composer John Williams, with music from Jurassic Park, Star Wars and more. Conductor Donato Cabrera makes his GTMF debut. Donato Cabrera, conductor. Buy Tickets

events.title: Free Family Jam: Heroes All Around Us

Fun for all ages — bring a picnic and enjoy the show, conducted by Timothy Constable and hosted by Stoner Family Education Curator Meaghan Heinrich . Timothy Constable, assistant conductor Meaghan Heinrich, host & Stoner Family Education Curator Maggie Berkenfield, vocalist Come early at 5 PM for an Instrument Petting Zoo led by the Jackson Hole Community Band!

events.title: Jayne & Al Hilde, Jr. Patriotic Pops
events.description: Festival favorite Capathia Jenkins returns to celebrate the Fourth of July with the Festival Orchestra in this festive Jackson Hole tradition. Conductor Donato Cabrera makes his GTMF debut, leading a program of patriotic & American music including Copland, Sousa, John Williams and more. Donato Cabrera, conductor Capathia Jenkins, vocalist Grab dinner from the food truck on site – Street Tacos and Jack’Stand! meet the artist Capathia Jenkins Vocalist Capathia Jenkins Vocalist The Brooklyn-born and raised singer/actor Capathia Jenkins is an accomplished vocalist, having performed on stages from Broadway to Carnegie Hall. Jenkins premiered her new show, ICON: The Voices That Changed Music, with the Princeton Symphony, with subsequent performances with the Houston Symphony, Baltimore Symphony, Reno Philharmonic, North Carolina Symphony, Anchorage Symphony and several others. She premiered her solo show, She’s Got Soul, with the Houston Symphony in October 2022, followed by performances with the Cleveland Orchestra, Seattle Symphony, Baltimore Symphony, Utah Symphony, Indianapolis Symphony, Naples Philharmonic and Philly Pops, among many others. Jenkins starred as “Medda” in the hit Disney production of Newsies on Broadway. She made her Broadway debut in The Civil War, where she created the role of Harriet Jackson. She then starred in the Off-Broadway 2000 revival of Godspell, where she wowed audiences with her stirring rendition of “Turn Back, O Man,” which can still be heard on the original cast recording. She returned to Broadway in The Look of Love and was critically acclaimed for her performances of the Bacharach/David hits. Jenkins then created the roles of “The Washing Machine” in Caroline, Or Change and “Frieda May” in Martin Short: Fame Becomes Me where she sang “Stop the Show” and brought the house down every night. In 2007 she went back to Off-Broadway and starred in (mis) Understanding Mammy: The Hattie McDaniel Story for which she was nominated for a Dram...
events.location: Center for the Arts Lawn, 265 S Cache Street, Jackson, WY, 83001
